name: Federal Old-Age and Survivors Insurance Trust Fund
agency: Social Security Administration (SSA)
description: Pays monthly retirement and survivors benefits to eligible workers and their families based on lifetime earnings. Funded primarily through the 6.2% OASDI payroll tax (employer and employee each) on earnings up to the taxable maximum.
beneficiaries: approximately 57 million retirees and survivors (FY2024)

name: Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund
agency: Department of Health and Human Services / Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (HHS/CMS)
description: Finances Medicare Part A, covering inpatient hospital stays, skilled nursing facility care, hospice, and some home health services for Americans aged 65+ and certain disabled individuals. Funded by the 1.45% Medicare payroll tax on all earnings (employer and employee each) plus a 0.9% Additional Medicare Tax on high earners.
beneficiaries: approximately 66 million Medicare enrollees (FY2024)

name: Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund
agency: Department of Health and Human Services / Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (HHS/CMS)
description: Finances Medicare Part B (physician and outpatient services) and Medicare Part D (prescription drug coverage). Funded approximately 75% by general revenues and 25% by beneficiary premiums.
beneficiaries: approximately 62 million Part B enrollees and 52 million Part D enrollees (FY2024)

name: Interest on the Public Debt
agency: Department of the Treasury / Bureau of the Fiscal Service
description: Pays interest on all outstanding Treasury securities (bills, notes, bonds, TIPS, and savings bonds) held by the public and intragovernmental accounts. This is a mandatory appropriation that covers the federal government's borrowing costs.
beneficiaries: not applicable (payments to bondholders and trust funds, not individual program beneficiaries)

name: Grants to States for Medicaid
agency: Department of Health and Human Services / Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (HHS/CMS)
description: Provides federal matching funds to states for the Medicaid program, which covers health care for low-income individuals, families, pregnant women, children, elderly, and people with disabilities. The federal share (FMAP) varies by state, ranging from 50% to about 77%.
beneficiaries: approximately 92 million Medicaid enrollees (FY2024)

name: Refunding Internal Revenue Collections
agency: Department of the Treasury / Internal Revenue Service (IRS)
description: Processes refunds of overpaid federal income taxes, payroll taxes, and related credits (including the Earned Income Tax Credit and Child Tax Credit) to individual and corporate taxpayers. This is a permanent, indefinite appropriation that fluctuates with filing activity and tax law changes.
beneficiaries: approximately 120 million individual tax refunds issued annually

name: Federal Disability Insurance Trust Fund
agency: Social Security Administration (SSA)
description: Pays monthly benefits to workers who become disabled before reaching full retirement age and to their eligible dependents. Funded by a dedicated 0.9% portion of the OASDI payroll tax (employer and employee each).
beneficiaries: approximately 7.4 million disabled workers plus 1.4 million dependents (FY2024)

name: Civil Service Retirement and Disability Fund
agency: Office of Personnel Management (OPM)
description: Funds retirement, disability, and survivor annuities for federal civilian employees under the Civil Service Retirement System (CSRS) and the Federal Employees Retirement System (FERS). Financed by employee and agency contributions plus a general fund transfer to cover the unfunded liability.
beneficiaries: approximately 2.7 million annuitants and survivors (FY2024)

name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
agency: Department of Agriculture / Food and Nutrition Service (USDA/FNS)
description: Provides monthly electronic benefits (EBT) to low-income households to purchase food, serving as the nation's largest domestic anti-hunger program. Eligibility is based on household income generally at or below 130% of the federal poverty level.
beneficiaries: approximately 42 million individuals in 22 million households (FY2024)

name: Military Personnel (Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Air Force, Space Force)
agency: Department of Defense (DoD)
description: Funds pay, allowances (housing, subsistence), bonuses, permanent change of station moves, and other compensation for active-duty military service members across all branches. Also covers Social Security and Medicare tax contributions for uniformed personnel.
beneficiaries: approximately 1.3 million active-duty service members

name: Operation and Maintenance (Army, Navy, Air Force, Defense-Wide)
agency: Department of Defense (DoD)
description: Finances day-to-day military operations, training, equipment maintenance, base operations, facilities sustainment, and civilian workforce pay across all service branches. The largest single discretionary appropriation category in the federal budget.
beneficiaries: not applicable (operational readiness of the armed forces)

name: Defense Working Capital Fund
agency: Department of Defense (DoD)
description: A revolving fund that operates on a business-like basis, providing goods and services (supply management, depot maintenance, transportation, logistics) to DoD components on a reimbursable basis. Activities set prices to recover costs, maintaining a self-sustaining cash flow cycle.
beneficiaries: not applicable (internal DoD supply chain and logistics)

name: Procurement (Aircraft, Missiles, Weapons, Shipbuilding, and Other â€” across services)
agency: Department of Defense (DoD)
treasury_account_symbol: various (21-2031 Aircraft Army, 17-1506 Weapons Navy, 57-3010 Aircraft AF, etc.)
description: Funds the acquisition of major weapons systems, aircraft, ships, vehicles, missiles, ammunition, and other military equipment for all service branches. Appropriations are typically available for three years (obligation) to accommodate multi-year production contracts.
beneficiaries: not applicable (equips the armed forces)

name: Government Payment for Annuitants, Employees Health Benefits
agency: Office of Personnel Management (OPM)
description: Pays the government's share (approximately 72%) of health insurance premiums for federal employees, retirees, and their dependents under the Federal Employees Health Benefits (FEHB) Program. Covers enrollment in a wide range of private health insurance plans.
beneficiaries: approximately 8.2 million federal employees, retirees, and dependents

name: Tenant-Based Rental Assistance (Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers)
agency: Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)
description: Provides portable housing vouchers to very low-income families, the elderly, and persons with disabilities to rent units in the private market, with HUD paying the difference between 30% of household income and the fair market rent. Administered by approximately 2,200 local public housing agencies.
beneficiaries: approximately 2.3 million households (about 5.2 million individuals)

name: Supplemental Security Income Program (SSI)
agency: Social Security Administration (SSA)
description: Provides monthly cash assistance to aged, blind, and disabled individuals with limited income and resources, establishing a uniform federal income floor. Funded entirely from general revenues (not the Social Security trust funds).
beneficiaries: approximately 7.5 million recipients (FY2024)

name: Federal Pell Grant Program
agency: Department of Education
description: Awards need-based grants to low-income undergraduate students to help pay for college tuition, fees, and living expenses, with no repayment required. Maximum award set annually by Congress (up to $7,395 in award year 2024-25).
beneficiaries: approximately 6.8 million undergraduate students annually

name: Student Financial Assistance (Federal Direct Loan Program)
agency: Department of Education
description: Administers the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan Program, issuing subsidized and unsubsidized loans, PLUS loans, and consolidation loans directly to students and parents for postsecondary education. Also includes costs related to income-driven repayment plans and loan forgiveness programs.
beneficiaries: approximately 43 million federal student loan borrowers with outstanding balances
fy2024_obligations_approx: $97 billion (gross loan disbursements; net subsidy cost varies)

name: Highway Trust Fund (Federal-Aid Highways / Federal Highway Administration)
agency: Department of Transportation / Federal Highway Administration (FHWA)
description: Finances federal-aid highway construction, maintenance, and safety improvement projects on the National Highway System and other eligible roads, distributed to states via formula and competitive grants. Funded primarily by the 18.4 cents/gallon federal gasoline tax, supplemented by general fund transfers.
beneficiaries: not applicable (benefits all highway users; funds distributed to 50 states, DC, territories, and tribal governments)

name: Defense Health Program (TRICARE)
agency: Department of Defense (DoD)
description: Provides medical and dental care to active-duty service members, retirees, and their dependents through the Military Health System, including direct care at military treatment facilities and purchased care through the TRICARE managed care network. Covers worldwide health services for the uniformed services community.
beneficiaries: approximately 9.6 million TRICARE-eligible beneficiaries

name: Medical Services / Medical Community Care (Veterans Health Administration)
agency: Department of Veterans Affairs (VA)
description: Funds inpatient, outpatient, mental health, long-term care, and community care (including the MISSION Act) health services for eligible veterans through the Veterans Health Administration's 171 medical centers and 1,100+ outpatient sites. Largest integrated health care system in the United States.
beneficiaries: approximately 9.1 million veterans enrolled in VA health care

name: Compensation and Pensions
agency: Department of Veterans Affairs (VA)
description: Provides tax-free disability compensation payments to veterans with service-connected injuries/illnesses and income-based pension benefits to wartime veterans who are permanently and totally disabled. Also includes dependency and indemnity compensation (DIC) for survivors of service members who died on duty or from service-connected causes.
beneficiaries: approximately 6.7 million veterans and survivors receiving compensation or pension

name: Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP)
agency: Department of Health and Human Services / Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (HHS/CMS)
description: Provides federal matching funds to states to cover uninsured children in families with incomes too high to qualify for Medicaid but too low to afford private coverage, with enhanced federal matching rates (generally 65%-85%). States design their programs as Medicaid expansions, separate CHIP programs, or combinations.
beneficiaries: approximately 7.4 million children and 370,000 pregnant women enrolled

name: Science / Exploration / Space Operations (NASA)
agency: National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)
description: Funds NASA's space science missions (James Webb Space Telescope, Mars exploration, Earth observation), human exploration programs (Artemis/Moon-to-Mars), and space operations (International Space Station, launch services, space communications). Supports the nation's civil space program and advances scientific understanding.
beneficiaries: not applicable (scientific research and exploration; supports thousands of research institutions, universities, and contractors)
fy2024_obligations_approx: $16 billion (Science ~$7.8B, Exploration ~$7.5B, Space Ops ~$4B â€” some overlap)

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Bureau of Investigation
agency: Department of Justice / Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
description: Funds FBI operations including counterterrorism, counterintelligence, cyber crime, criminal investigations, intelligence collection, and forensic laboratory services across 56 field offices and 350+ resident agencies nationwide. Also supports international operations through legal attache offices in over 60 countries.
beneficiaries: not applicable (federal law enforcement; approximately 37,000 employees)

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Prison System (Bureau of Prisons)
agency: Department of Justice / Bureau of Prisons (BOP)
description: Operates and maintains 122 federal correctional institutions of varying security levels, plus residential reentry centers, housing federal inmates convicted of federal offenses. Covers inmate care, custody, security, education, vocational training, medical services, and community corrections.
beneficiaries: approximately 158,000 federal inmates in BOP custody

name: Salaries and Expenses, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
agency: Department of Homeland Security / Customs and Border Protection (CBP)
description: Funds border security operations including Border Patrol agents, officers at ports of entry, trade enforcement, agricultural inspections, and anti-smuggling operations along the nation's borders and at 328 ports of entry. Employs the largest federal law enforcement workforce in the United States.
beneficiaries: not applicable (border security; approximately 63,000 employees including ~20,000 Border Patrol agents and ~27,000 CBP officers)

name: Disaster Relief Fund
agency: Department of Homeland Security / Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA)
description: Provides federal assistance following presidential disaster declarations, funding individual assistance (housing, personal property), public assistance (infrastructure repair for state/local governments), and hazard mitigation grants. The primary funding source for federal disaster response and recovery operations.
beneficiaries: varies widely by year; millions of individuals and thousands of state/local government entities and nonprofits affected by declared disasters

name: National Institutes of Health (NIH â€” consolidated across 27 Institutes and Centers)
agency: Department of Health and Human Services / National Institutes of Health (HHS/NIH)
description: Funds biomedical and public health research through intramural labs and extramural grants to universities, medical schools, and research institutions worldwide, covering basic science, clinical trials, and translational research across all major diseases. The world's largest public funder of biomedical research.
beneficiaries: not applicable (research enterprise; funds approximately 50,000 competitive grants to over 2,500 institutions)

name: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)
agency: Department of Health and Human Services / CDC
description: Funds disease surveillance, outbreak investigation, immunization programs, chronic disease prevention, injury prevention, environmental health, and global health security activities to protect the nation from health threats. Provides critical grants and cooperative agreements to state and local health departments.
beneficiaries: not applicable (public health infrastructure; grants to all 50 states, territories, and hundreds of local health departments)

name: Forest Service (National Forest System, Wildland Fire Management, State and Private Forestry)
agency: Department of Agriculture / U.S. Forest Service (USDA/FS)
description: Manages 193 million acres of national forests and grasslands for multiple uses including timber, recreation, watershed protection, and wildlife habitat, and leads federal wildland firefighting operations. Wildland fire suppression and fuels management constitute the largest share of the Forest Service budget.
beneficiaries: not applicable (public lands management; serves approximately 160 million recreational visitors annually)

name: Weapons Activities, National Nuclear Security Administration
agency: Department of Energy / National Nuclear Security Administration (DOE/NNSA)
description: Maintains the safety, security, and reliability of the U.S. nuclear weapons stockpile through the Stockpile Stewardship Program, without underground testing, and manages nuclear weapons production and infrastructure modernization at sites including Los Alamos, Sandia, and Lawrence Livermore. Also supports nuclear nonproliferation, naval reactors, and emergency response.
beneficiaries: not applicable (national security; operates through seven national laboratories and production plants)

name: Environmental Management (Defense Environmental Cleanup)
agency: Department of Energy (DOE)
description: Cleans up the environmental legacy of Cold War-era nuclear weapons production and government-sponsored nuclear energy research across the DOE complex, including radioactive waste treatment, spent fuel management, and facility decommissioning. The world's largest environmental remediation program.
beneficiaries: not applicable (environmental remediation at 16 major sites across 12 states; protects surrounding communities and ecosystems)

name: Operations, Federal Aviation Administration
agency: Department of Transportation / Federal Aviation Administration (FAA)
description: Funds the operation of the national airspace system, including air traffic control at approximately 300 FAA-operated facilities, aviation safety regulation and certification, and the employment of approximately 14,000 air traffic controllers. Also covers Facilities & Equipment and Research, Engineering & Development accounts.
beneficiaries: not applicable (aviation safety; manages approximately 45,000 flights per day in U.S. airspace)

name: School Improvement Programs (Supporting Effective Instruction / Title II; 21st Century Community Learning Centers; other ESEA programs)
agency: Department of Education
description: Provides formula and competitive grants to states and school districts under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act (ESEA/ESSA) for teacher quality improvement, after-school programs, charter schools, magnet schools, and other school improvement initiatives. Complements the larger Title I grants to local educational agencies.
beneficiaries: approximately 25,000 school districts and 100,000 public schools serving millions of students

name: Special Education (Grants to States under IDEA Part B)
agency: Department of Education / Office of Special Education Programs
description: Provides formula grants to states to assist in providing free appropriate public education (FAPE) to children and youth with disabilities ages 3-21 under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA). Represents the federal share of the excess cost of educating students with disabilities.
beneficiaries: approximately 7.5 million students with disabilities receiving special education services

name: Community Development Fund (Community Development Block Grants â€” CDBG)
agency: Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)
description: Provides flexible annual formula grants to entitled cities (population 50,000+), urban counties, and states for community development activities directed toward neighborhood revitalization, economic development, and improved community facilities and services benefiting low- and moderate-income persons. At least 70% of funds must benefit LMI populations.
beneficiaries: approximately 1,200 entitled communities and all 50 states; benefits millions of LMI residents

name: Operation of Indian Programs / Indian Affairs
agency: Department of the Interior / Bureau of Indian Affairs (BIA) and Bureau of Indian Education (BIE)
description: Funds trust responsibilities to 574 federally recognized tribes, including tribal governance, natural resources management, law enforcement, social services, and education through 183 BIE-funded schools. Supports tribal self-determination and sovereignty through direct services, contracts, and self-governance compacts.
beneficiaries: approximately 1.9 million American Indians and Alaska Natives who are members of federally recognized tribes

name: Resource Management, U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service
agency: Department of the Interior / U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (FWS)
description: Manages the National Wildlife Refuge System (568 refuges on 850 million acres), administers the Endangered Species Act, enforces federal wildlife laws, manages migratory bird conservation, and operates national fish hatcheries. Also administers grants to states for wildlife and sport fish restoration.
beneficiaries: not applicable (wildlife conservation; refuges host approximately 65 million visitors annually)

name: Operation of the National Park System
agency: Department of the Interior / National Park Service (NPS)
description: Operates and maintains 429 units of the National Park System, including national parks, monuments, historic sites, recreation areas, seashores, and battlefields, preserving natural and cultural resources while providing for public enjoyment. Also administers grant and partnership programs for historic preservation and recreation.
beneficiaries: not applicable (public lands; approximately 312 million recreational visits annually)

name: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers â€” Civil Works (Investigations, Construction, Operation and Maintenance)
agency: Department of Defense â€” Civil / U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE)
description: Plans, designs, builds, and operates water resources infrastructure including navigation channels, flood risk management systems (levees, dams, floodwalls), hydropower facilities, aquatic ecosystem restoration, and regulatory permits for wetlands and waterways. Manages over 700 dams and 12,000 miles of inland waterways.
beneficiaries: not applicable (water infrastructure; benefits communities nationwide, especially in flood-prone areas and along navigable waterways)

name: Foreign Military Financing
agency: Department of State / Department of Defense (administered by Defense Security Cooperation Agency)
description: Provides grants (and historically loans) to foreign governments for the purchase of U.S. defense articles, defense services, and military training, with the majority of funding historically going to Israel, Egypt, Jordan, and other strategic partners. Advances U.S. national security objectives through allied capacity building and interoperability.
beneficiaries: not applicable (approximately 50+ partner countries; Israel receives approximately $3.3 billion annually under the 2016 MOU)

name: Economic Support Fund
agency: Department of State / U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID)
description: Provides economic assistance to countries of strategic importance to U.S. foreign policy, supporting governance, rule of law, economic reforms, education, health, and stabilization programs. Grants are flexible and can address a wide range of development and diplomatic objectives.
beneficiaries: not applicable (foreign assistance to approximately 70+ countries and territories)

name: Global Health Programs (PEPFAR/Global Health Security)
agency: Department of State / Office of the Global AIDS Coordinator; HHS/CDC Global; USAID Global Health
description: Funds the President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief (PEPFAR) and global health initiatives addressing HIV/AIDS, malaria (PMI), tuberculosis, maternal and child health, family planning, nutrition, and pandemic preparedness in developing countries. PEPFAR alone has supported antiretroviral treatment for over 20 million people.
beneficiaries: not applicable (global health; PEPFAR supports HIV treatment for approximately 20.1 million people in 50+ countries)
fy2024_obligations_approx: $10.6 billion (across State, USAID, CDC, and DoD global health accounts)

name: Disaster Loans Program Account, Small Business Administration
agency: Small Business Administration (SBA)
description: Provides low-interest, long-term loans to homeowners, renters, businesses of all sizes, and private nonprofits to repair or replace property damaged by declared disasters, including real estate, personal property, machinery, and inventory. The primary form of federal disaster assistance for non-agricultural private-sector losses.
beneficiaries: varies by year based on disaster activity; typically tens of thousands of disaster loan approvals annually
fy2024_obligations_approx: $2.5 billion (varies significantly based on disaster activity)

name: Rural Development Programs (Rural Housing Service, Rural Utilities Service, Rural Business-Cooperative Service)
agency: Department of Agriculture / Rural Development (USDA/RD)
description: Provides loans, loan guarantees, and grants for rural housing (single-family and multi-family), water and waste disposal systems, electric and telecommunications infrastructure, broadband, and rural business development. Serves communities with populations generally under 35,000.
beneficiaries: approximately 60 million people living in rural America; specific program beneficiaries include hundreds of thousands of homeowners and thousands of communities
fy2024_obligations_approx: $5.5 billion (program level much higher due to loan guarantees)

name: Federal Transit Administration (Transit Formula Grants and Capital Investment Grants)
agency: Department of Transportation / Federal Transit Administration (FTA)
description: Provides formula and competitive grants to state and local transit agencies for capital investments, operations, planning, and safety improvements for public transportation systems including buses, subways, light rail, commuter rail, and ferries. The Bipartisan Infrastructure Law significantly increased authorized funding levels.
beneficiaries: not applicable (public transit systems; approximately 900+ transit agencies serving millions of daily riders in urban and rural areas)

name: National Railroad Passenger Corporation Grants (Amtrak)
agency: Department of Transportation / Federal Railroad Administration (FRA)
description: Provides annual federal grants to Amtrak for the operation, maintenance, and capital improvement of intercity passenger rail service across a 21,000-mile national network serving 500+ stations in 46 states. The Bipartisan Infrastructure Law provided $66 billion over five years for passenger and freight rail.
beneficiaries: approximately 28.6 million Amtrak riders annually (FY2024 ridership)

name: Hazardous Substance Superfund (EPA Superfund Program)
agency: Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)
description: Funds the investigation and cleanup of the nation's most contaminated hazardous waste sites (National Priorities List), emergency response to hazardous substance releases, and enforcement actions against responsible parties. The Bipartisan Infrastructure Law provided an additional $3.5 billion for Superfund cleanups.
beneficiaries: not applicable (environmental cleanup; approximately 1,340 NPL sites with 73 million Americans living within three miles of a Superfund site)

name: Research and Related Activities, National Science Foundation
agency: National Science Foundation (NSF)
description: Funds fundamental research and education across all fields of science and engineering (excluding medical sciences) through competitive merit-reviewed grants to universities and research institutions, supporting approximately 25% of all federally funded basic research at academic institutions. Also funds major research facilities, STEM workforce development, and international science cooperation.
beneficiaries: not applicable (research enterprise; approximately 50,000 awards to 2,000+ institutions annually; supports approximately 300,000 researchers, postdocs, and students)
fy2024_obligations_approx: $9.1 billion (Research and Related Activities only; total NSF approximately $11.3 billion)

name: Weapons Activities, National Nuclear Security Administration, Department of Energy
description: Maintains the safety, security, and effectiveness of the U.S. nuclear weapons stockpile without underground testing. Funds warhead life extension programs, stockpile stewardship, and nuclear weapons production infrastructure.
beneficiaries: U.S. national defense and nuclear deterrence infrastructure

name: Federal Crop Insurance Corporation Fund
description: Provides subsidized crop insurance to American farmers and ranchers to protect against losses from natural disasters, price declines, and revenue shortfalls. Administered by the Risk Management Agency within USDA.
beneficiaries: U.S. farmers, ranchers, and agricultural producers

name: Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation Fund
description: Insures private-sector defined benefit pension plans and pays benefits when underfunded plans are terminated. Created by ERISA, it is funded by insurance premiums, investment income, and assets of failed plans.
beneficiaries: Workers and retirees in private-sector defined benefit pension plans

name: Operations and Support, Customs and Border Protection, Department of Homeland Security
description: Funds CBP's primary mission of securing U.S. borders at and between ports of entry, including personnel, technology, and infrastructure for border security. Supports trade enforcement, immigration inspection, and agricultural protection.
beneficiaries: General public, lawful travelers, and U.S. trade community

name: Shipbuilding and Conversion, Navy
description: Finances the construction, acquisition, and conversion of naval vessels including aircraft carriers, submarines, destroyers, and amphibious ships. Provides long-lead-time materials and detail design for new ship classes.
beneficiaries: U.S. Navy, defense shipbuilding industrial base

name: State Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP)
description: Provides federal matching funds to states for health insurance coverage of uninsured children in families with incomes too high for Medicaid but too low to afford private coverage. Created by the Balanced Budget Act of 1997.
beneficiaries: Low-income children and pregnant women not eligible for Medicaid

name: Research, Development, Test and Evaluation, Space Force
description: Funds R&D for space-based military capabilities including satellite communications, missile warning, space domain awareness, and GPS modernization. Established when the Space Force was created as the sixth military branch.
beneficiaries: U.S. Space Force, Department of Defense, space technology industrial base

name: Concurrent Receipt Accrual Payments to the DoD Military Retirement Fund
description: Funds concurrent receipt of military retired pay and VA disability compensation for qualifying military retirees with service-connected disabilities. Authorized by the FY2004 NDAA to end the longstanding offset between the two benefits.
beneficiaries: Military retirees with service-connected disabilities rated 50% or higher

name: Cost of War Toxic Exposures Fund, Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Created by the PACT Act to fund health care and benefits for veterans exposed to burn pits, Agent Orange, and other toxic substances during military service. Provides mandatory funding outside normal VA discretionary caps.
beneficiaries: Veterans exposed to toxic substances during military service

name: Education for the Disadvantaged (Title I Grants)
description: Provides formula grants to school districts with high concentrations of low-income students to help ensure all children meet challenging academic standards. Authorized under Title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act.
beneficiaries: Low-income K-12 students, high-poverty school districts

name: Aircraft Procurement, Air Force
description: Funds procurement of fighter, bomber, tanker, transport, trainer, and special mission aircraft plus modifications and spares for the Air Force. Covers production of platforms such as the F-35A, KC-46, and B-21.
beneficiaries: U.S. Air Force, aerospace defense industrial base

name: Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF)
description: Provides block grants to states for cash assistance, work programs, and support services for low-income families with children. Replaced the AFDC entitlement program under the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Act of 1996.
beneficiaries: Low-income families with dependent children

name: Project-Based Rental Assistance, Department of Housing and Urban Development
description: Provides rental subsidy payments to private property owners under long-term contracts to maintain affordable housing units for low-income tenants. Contracts are tied to specific housing projects rather than following individual tenants.
beneficiaries: Low-income renters in privately owned subsidized housing

name: Military Personnel, Marine Corps
description: Funds pay, allowances, bonuses, and benefits for active-duty Marine Corps personnel including basic pay, housing allowances, subsistence, and special/incentive pays. Covers the end strength of approximately 172,000–180,000 active Marines.
beneficiaries: Active-duty U.S. Marines and their dependents

name: Special Education (IDEA Grants to States)
description: Provides formula grants to states and territories to assist in providing free appropriate public education to children with disabilities ages 3–21. Authorized under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act.
beneficiaries: Children and youth with disabilities, school districts serving them

name: Aircraft Procurement, Navy
description: Funds the acquisition of fixed-wing and rotary-wing aircraft for the Navy and Marine Corps, including the F-35B/C, MV-22, CH-53K, and E-2D. Includes spare engines, modifications, and support equipment.
beneficiaries: U.S. Navy and Marine Corps aviation, defense aerospace industry

name: Children and Families Services Programs (Head Start and Child Care)
description: Funds Head Start/Early Head Start preschool programs for low-income children and the Child Care and Development Block Grant. Provides comprehensive child development services including education, health, nutrition, and parent engagement.
beneficiaries: Low-income children ages 0-5, working families needing child care

name: Government Payment for Annuitants, Employee Health Benefits
description: Pays the federal government's share of health insurance premiums for retired federal civilian employees and their survivors enrolled in the Federal Employees Health Benefits (FEHB) program. Functions as the employer contribution for retiree health coverage.
beneficiaries: Retired federal civilian employees and their survivors

name: Formula and Bus Grants, Federal Transit Administration
description: Provides formula-based capital and operating assistance to transit agencies for bus and rail systems, including urbanized area formula grants and bus/bus facility programs. The largest FTA program supporting public transportation nationwide.
beneficiaries: Public transit agencies, transit riders in urban and rural areas

name: Other Procurement, Navy
description: Funds procurement of equipment and material not covered by dedicated shipbuilding or aircraft accounts, including communications, electronics, ordnance, tactical vehicles, and support equipment. Covers a wide range of Navy combat and logistics systems.
beneficiaries: U.S. Navy fleet operations and readiness

name: Limitation on Administrative Expenses, Social Security Administration
description: Funds SSA's operating costs including processing Social Security and SSI claims, maintaining earnings records, issuing Social Security numbers, and administering disability determinations. Became an independent agency account in 1994.
beneficiaries: Social Security and SSI beneficiaries, general public using SSA services

name: Readjustment Benefits, Veterans Benefits Administration (GI Bill)
description: Provides education and training benefits to veterans and eligible dependents, primarily through the Post-9/11 GI Bill (Chapter 33) and vocational rehabilitation. Covers tuition, housing allowances, and book stipends.
beneficiaries: Post-9/11 veterans, service members, and eligible dependents pursuing education

name: Federal Family Education Loan Program Account
description: Manages the legacy portfolio of federally guaranteed student loans originated by private lenders before the program's termination in 2010. Covers subsidy costs, default claims, and loan consolidation for the remaining portfolio.
beneficiaries: Student loan borrowers with legacy FFEL loans

name: Federal Buildings Fund, General Services Administration
description: A revolving fund that finances the construction, acquisition, renovation, and management of federally owned and leased buildings. Revenue comes from rent collected from tenant federal agencies.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies occupying GSA-managed buildings

name: Operations, Federal Aviation Administration
description: Funds FAA's air traffic control operations, aviation safety regulation, certification of aircraft and pilots, and maintenance of the national airspace system. Supports over 300 air traffic control facilities and approximately 26,000 controllers.
beneficiaries: Commercial airlines, general aviation, flying public

name: Revolving Fund, Corps of Engineers, Civil Works
description: Finances the Army Corps of Engineers' reimbursable civil works activities including design, construction, and repair work performed for other federal agencies and non-federal entities. Replenished through payments from customers.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies and non-federal sponsors of civil works projects

name: Department of Defense Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund
description: Finances TRICARE health benefits for Medicare-eligible military retirees and their dependents through an accrual-based fund established by the FY2001 NDAA. Ensures long-term funding of retiree health obligations.
beneficiaries: Medicare-eligible military retirees, dependents, and survivors

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Bureau of Investigation
description: Funds the FBI's law enforcement, intelligence, and national security operations including counterterrorism, cybercrime, public corruption, and organized crime investigations. Supports approximately 35,000 personnel across 56 field offices and international legal attaché offices.
beneficiaries: General public, federal law enforcement community

name: Diplomatic and Consular Programs, Department of State
description: Funds the Department of State's core diplomatic operations including staffing and operating approximately 270 embassies, consulates, and diplomatic missions worldwide. Covers personnel, diplomatic security, information technology, and public diplomacy.
beneficiaries: U.S. foreign policy establishment, American citizens abroad

name: Global Health Programs, Department of State (PEPFAR)
description: Funds the President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief and other global health initiatives combating HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, malaria, and pandemic preparedness. PEPFAR is credited with saving over 25 million lives in sub-Saharan Africa and other regions.
beneficiaries: Populations in developing countries affected by HIV/AIDS and infectious diseases

name: Operation and Maintenance, Marine Corps
description: Funds the Marine Corps' day-to-day operating costs including training, equipment maintenance, base operations, logistics, and civilian personnel. Ensures readiness for expeditionary and amphibious combat operations.
beneficiaries: U.S. Marine Corps operational forces and installations

name: National Guard Personnel, Army
description: Funds pay, allowances, and benefits for Army National Guard members during drill weekends, annual training, and active-duty periods. Covers approximately 325,000–336,000 Guard members in 54 states and territories.
beneficiaries: Army National Guard members and their dependents

name: Medical Support and Compliance, Veterans Health Administration
description: Funds administrative, compliance, quality management, and support functions for VA health care facilities including finance, human resources, security, and regulatory compliance. Supports the operational infrastructure of the VA health system.
beneficiaries: Veterans receiving VA health care through VHA facilities

name: Operating Expenses, United States Coast Guard
description: Funds Coast Guard operations including search and rescue, maritime law enforcement, drug interdiction, port security, environmental protection, and aids to navigation. Supports the operation of cutters, aircraft, and shore units.
beneficiaries: Maritime community, coastal populations, general public

name: Operations and Support, Transportation Security Administration
description: Funds TSA's aviation security screening operations at approximately 440 federally regulated airports, including passenger and baggage screening. Created after 9/11 within the Aviation and Transportation Security Act.
beneficiaries: Air travelers, aviation industry, general public

name: Community Development Fund (CDBG), Department of Housing and Urban Development
description: Provides flexible formula grants to states and local communities for housing rehabilitation, infrastructure improvements, public services, and economic development in low- and moderate-income areas. Authorized under Title I of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974.
beneficiaries: Low- and moderate-income communities, local governments

name: Commodity Credit Corporation Fund, USDA
description: Finances USDA farm price and income support programs, commodity purchases, export subsidies, and conservation payments to agricultural producers. Functions as a revolving fund with borrowing authority from the U.S. Treasury.
beneficiaries: U.S. agricultural producers, commodity exporters

name: General Operating Expenses, Veterans Benefits Administration
description: Funds VBA's administrative costs for processing veterans' disability compensation, pension, education, home loan, insurance, and burial benefit claims. Supports approximately 25,000 VBA employees across regional offices.
beneficiaries: Veterans and dependents applying for VA benefits

name: Foster Care and Permanency Payments (Title IV-E)
description: Provides federal matching payments to states for foster care maintenance, adoption assistance, and kinship guardianship assistance for eligible children removed from their homes. Authorized under Title IV-E of the Social Security Act.
beneficiaries: Children in foster care, adoptive families, kinship caregivers

name: Other Procurement, Army
description: Funds Army procurement of tactical and commercial vehicles, communications/electronics equipment, night vision devices, chemical defense equipment, and other support items. Covers a broad range of equipment not included in dedicated weapon system accounts.
beneficiaries: U.S. Army combat and support units

name: Operation and Maintenance, Army National Guard
description: Funds the day-to-day operating costs of Army National Guard units including training, equipment maintenance, facility operations, and civilian technician pay. Supports readiness of Guard units for both federal and state missions.
beneficiaries: Army National Guard units in 54 states and territories

name: Operations and Support, Immigration and Customs Enforcement
description: Funds ICE's immigration enforcement, detention operations, removal proceedings, and Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) programs. Covers detention bed space, transportation, and investigations of transnational crime.
beneficiaries: General public, immigration enforcement operations

name: Exchange Stabilization Fund, Department of the Treasury
description: Provides the Treasury Secretary with resources to intervene in foreign exchange markets to stabilize the value of the dollar. Established by the Gold Reserve Act of 1934, also used for emergency financial stabilization.
beneficiaries: U.S. economy, international monetary stability

name: Science, Energy Programs, Department of Energy
description: Funds basic research in physics, chemistry, biology, environmental sciences, and advanced computing through DOE's national laboratory system. Supports 17 national laboratories and is the largest federal sponsor of basic research in the physical sciences.
beneficiaries: Scientific research community, national laboratories, universities

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Prison System, Bureau of Prisons
description: Funds operation of the federal prison system including staff salaries, inmate care, food, medical services, and facility maintenance at over 120 federal correctional institutions. Manages approximately 155,000 federal inmates.
beneficiaries: Federal inmates, correctional staff, public safety

name: Missile Procurement, Army
description: Funds procurement of Army missile systems including Patriot, THAAD, Javelin, Stinger, GMLRS, and associated ground support equipment. Covers production, modification, and initial spares for air and missile defense and tactical missile systems.
beneficiaries: U.S. Army air defense and tactical missile units

name: Procurement, Marine Corps
description: Funds procurement of Marine Corps ground combat and support equipment including amphibious combat vehicles, ground vehicles, communications systems, and individual combat equipment. Covers items not procured through Navy aviation or shipbuilding accounts.
beneficiaries: U.S. Marine Corps ground combat forces

name: Defense Nuclear Nonproliferation, NNSA
description: Funds programs to detect, prevent, and reverse the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction, including securing vulnerable nuclear materials worldwide. Supports nuclear forensics, arms control verification, and international nonproliferation cooperation.
beneficiaries: Global nuclear security, international nonproliferation regime

name: Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children (WIC)
description: Provides supplemental nutritious foods, nutrition education, and health care referrals to low-income pregnant women, new mothers, infants, and children up to age five. Serves approximately 6–7 million participants monthly.
beneficiaries: Low-income pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children under 5

name: Operation of Indian Programs, Bureau of Indian Affairs
description: Funds the operation of Bureau of Indian Affairs programs including tribal government support, law enforcement, social services, natural resources management, and education on Indian reservations. Serves 574 federally recognized tribes.
beneficiaries: Federally recognized American Indian and Alaska Native tribes and members

name: Military Construction, Army
description: Funds planning, design, and construction of Army facilities including barracks, training ranges, maintenance shops, headquarters buildings, and installation infrastructure. Covers both stateside and overseas construction projects.
beneficiaries: U.S. Army installations and personnel

name: Military Construction, Navy and Marine Corps
description: Funds construction of Navy and Marine Corps shore facilities including piers, hangars, training facilities, and operational support buildings. Supports the Navy's infrastructure requirements at bases worldwide.
beneficiaries: U.S. Navy and Marine Corps installations and personnel

name: Military Construction, Air Force
description: Funds planning, design, and construction of Air Force facilities including runways, aircraft shelters, munitions storage, and mission support buildings. Supports the infrastructure needs of Air Force bases worldwide.
beneficiaries: U.S. Air Force installations and personnel

name: Salaries and Expenses, Drug Enforcement Administration
description: Funds DEA's enforcement of federal controlled substances laws, including drug trafficking investigations, diversion control, intelligence, and international operations. Supports approximately 10,000 employees including special agents in domestic and foreign offices.
beneficiaries: General public, drug enforcement community

name: Salaries and Expenses, Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives
description: Funds ATF's law enforcement operations including firearms trafficking investigations, explosives regulation, arson investigations, and alcohol/tobacco tax enforcement. Administers federal firearms licensing and the National Tracing Center.
beneficiaries: General public, law enforcement community

name: Salaries and Expenses, United States Marshals Service
description: Funds the oldest federal law enforcement agency's operations including fugitive apprehension, judicial security, witness protection, prisoner transport, and asset forfeiture. Protects approximately 2,700 federal judges and judicial facilities.
beneficiaries: Federal judiciary, justice system, general public

name: Operations and Support, United States Secret Service
description: Funds the Secret Service's dual mission of protecting national leaders and investigating financial crimes including counterfeiting, cyber fraud, and identity theft. Protects the President, Vice President, and other designated officials.
beneficiaries: Protected officials and families, financial system integrity

name: Federal Emergency Management Agency Grants and Training
description: Funds FEMA's non-disaster grant programs including Homeland Security Grant Program, State Homeland Security Program, Urban Area Security Initiative, and firefighter assistance grants. Strengthens state and local preparedness capabilities.
beneficiaries: State and local governments, first responders, emergency management agencies

name: Hazardous Substance Superfund, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Funds EPA's cleanup of the nation's most contaminated hazardous waste sites on the National Priorities List. Authorized under CERCLA (Superfund law) to address legacy pollution threats to human health and the environment.
beneficiaries: Communities near contaminated sites, public health and environment

name: State and Tribal Assistance Grants, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Provides grants to states and tribes for environmental programs including clean water and drinking water state revolving funds, categorical grants, and brownfields cleanup. The largest EPA grant program supporting state environmental infrastructure.
beneficiaries: State and tribal environmental agencies, communities with water infrastructure needs

name: National Cancer Institute, NIH
description: The largest NIH institute, funding cancer research including basic science, clinical trials, cancer prevention, and treatment development. Supports intramural research and extramural grants to universities and research centers nationwide.
beneficiaries: Cancer patients, biomedical research community, public health

name: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases, NIH
description: Funds research on infectious, immunologic, and allergic diseases including HIV/AIDS, emerging infectious diseases, biodefense, and vaccine development. Played a central role in COVID-19 vaccine and therapeutic development.
beneficiaries: Patients with infectious and immune diseases, global health

name: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute, NIH
description: Funds research on cardiovascular, lung, and blood diseases and disorders, including clinical trials and population studies. Supports landmark studies such as the Framingham Heart Study.
beneficiaries: Patients with heart, lung, and blood disorders; biomedical researchers

name: National Institute of General Medical Sciences, NIH
description: Funds basic biomedical research that is not targeted at specific diseases, including cell biology, genetics, pharmacology, and computational biology. Supports research training and institutional development grants.
beneficiaries: Basic science research community, future biomedical workforce

name: National Forest System, Forest Service, USDA
description: Funds management and protection of 193 million acres of national forests and grasslands, including wildfire prevention, recreation, timber, watershed, and wildlife habitat management. Supports approximately 30,000 Forest Service employees.
beneficiaries: Forest visitors, rural communities, timber and recreation industries

name: Wildland Fire Management, Forest Service, USDA
description: Funds wildfire suppression, hazardous fuels reduction, and fire preparedness activities on national forests and adjacent lands. One of the fastest-growing federal expenditures due to increasing wildfire severity.
beneficiaries: Communities in wildland-urban interface, forest ecosystems

name: Management of Lands and Resources, Bureau of Land Management
description: Funds BLM's management of approximately 245 million acres of public lands, including rangeland management, energy/mineral leasing, recreation, and conservation. Manages more surface land than any other federal agency.
beneficiaries: Ranchers, energy producers, recreationists, conservation interests

name: Resource Management, United States Fish and Wildlife Service
description: Funds FWS operations including endangered species conservation, migratory bird management, national wildlife refuge system operations, and law enforcement. Manages over 560 national wildlife refuges.
beneficiaries: Endangered and threatened species, wildlife refuge visitors, conservation community

name: Operation of the National Park System, National Park Service
description: Funds the operation, maintenance, and management of over 420 units in the National Park System, including visitor services, resource protection, law enforcement, and facility maintenance. Hosts over 300 million visits annually.
beneficiaries: Park visitors, cultural and natural resource preservation

name: Operations, Research, and Facilities, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
description: Funds NOAA's core operations including the National Weather Service, ocean and fisheries research, satellite operations, and coastal zone management. Provides weather forecasts, climate data, and marine resource management.
beneficiaries: General public, mariners, aviation, agriculture, fisheries industry

name: Periodic Censuses and Programs, Census Bureau
description: Funds the decennial census, American Community Survey, economic census, and other periodic surveys conducted by the Census Bureau. Constitutionally mandated for congressional apportionment and widely used for federal fund distribution.
beneficiaries: Federal, state, and local governments; researchers; businesses

name: Salaries and Expenses, United States Patent and Trademark Office
description: Funds the examination and issuance of patents and trademarks, operating almost entirely on user fees from applicants. Processes approximately 600,000 patent applications and 900,000 trademark applications annually.
beneficiaries: Inventors, businesses, intellectual property holders

name: Water and Related Resources, Bureau of Reclamation
description: Funds operation, maintenance, and construction of water infrastructure projects in the 17 western states, including dams, reservoirs, canals, and hydropower facilities. Manages water delivery to over 31 million people and 10 million acres of farmland.
beneficiaries: Western farmers, municipalities, hydropower consumers, recreation users

name: Bonneville Power Administration Fund
description: A self-financing federal power marketing administration that markets wholesale electricity from 31 federal dams and one nuclear plant in the Pacific Northwest. Operates approximately 15,000 miles of high-voltage transmission lines.
beneficiaries: Pacific Northwest electric utilities, ratepayers, and industries

name: Tennessee Valley Authority Fund
description: A federally owned corporation providing electricity generation, flood control, navigation, and economic development across the Tennessee Valley region covering parts of seven states. One of the nation's largest public power providers.
beneficiaries: Tennessee Valley residents and businesses across seven states

name: Postal Service Fund
description: Accounts for the revenues and expenditures of the United States Postal Service, an independent federal establishment that operates primarily from postal rate revenues. Delivers to approximately 167 million addresses.
beneficiaries: General public, businesses, mailers

name: Railroad Retirement Board, Benefit Payments
description: Administers retirement, disability, unemployment, and sickness benefits for railroad workers and their families under a separate system from Social Security. Covers approximately 500,000 beneficiaries.
beneficiaries: Retired and disabled railroad workers and their families

name: Foreign Military Financing Program, Department of State
description: Provides grants and loans to foreign governments to purchase U.S. defense equipment, services, and training to support U.S. national security objectives. Israel and Egypt are historically the largest recipients.
beneficiaries: Allied and partner foreign governments, U.S. defense industry

name: Economic Support Fund, Department of State/USAID
description: Provides economic assistance to countries of strategic importance to the United States, funding governance, economic growth, education, and stabilization programs. Supports U.S. foreign policy objectives in key regions.
beneficiaries: Developing countries and fragile states of strategic importance to the U.S.

name: International Disaster Assistance, USAID
description: Funds U.S. government humanitarian assistance for international disasters including emergency food, shelter, medical care, and disaster risk reduction. Responds to natural disasters, complex emergencies, and refugee crises worldwide.
beneficiaries: Populations affected by international disasters and humanitarian crises

name: Peace Corps
description: Sends American volunteers to developing countries for two-year service assignments in education, health, agriculture, environment, and community development. Has sent over 240,000 volunteers to more than 140 countries since inception.
beneficiaries: Communities in developing countries, American volunteers

name: Operating Expenses, United States Agency for International Development
description: Funds USAID's administrative and operating costs for managing U.S. international development and humanitarian programs in over 100 countries. Covers staff salaries, overseas mission operations, and program management.
beneficiaries: Developing countries, U.S. foreign assistance programs

name: Export-Import Bank of the United States
description: Provides export credit financing including direct loans, guarantees, and insurance to support U.S. exports when private financing is unavailable. Operates on a self-sustaining basis using fees and interest income.
beneficiaries: U.S. exporters, particularly small businesses, and foreign buyers of U.S. goods

name: Disaster Loans Program Account, Small Business Administration
description: Provides low-interest direct loans to businesses, homeowners, and renters to repair or replace property damaged by declared disasters. The primary federal disaster loan program for non-farm private sector losses.
beneficiaries: Disaster-affected homeowners, renters, and businesses

name: Rural Housing Insurance Fund, USDA
description: Provides direct and guaranteed loans for single-family and multi-family housing in rural areas with populations under 35,000. Supports homeownership and rental housing through the Section 502 and Section 515 programs.
beneficiaries: Low- and moderate-income rural homebuyers and renters

name: Rural Utilities Service, USDA
description: Provides loans, grants, and loan guarantees for rural electric, telecommunications, water, and waste disposal infrastructure. Originally created as the Rural Electrification Administration to bring electricity to rural America.
beneficiaries: Rural electric cooperatives, telephone companies, water systems

name: Federal-Aid Highways, Federal Highway Administration (Obligation Limitation)
description: The primary federal program funding construction, reconstruction, and maintenance of the National Highway System and other federal-aid roads through formula grants to states. Funded largely through the Highway Trust Fund from fuel taxes.
beneficiaries: State transportation departments, motorists, commercial freight industry

name: Grants-in-Aid for Airports (Airport Improvement Program), FAA
description: Provides formula and discretionary grants to public-use airports for planning, construction, and rehabilitation of airport infrastructure including runways, taxiways, and safety improvements. Funded by the Airport and Airway Trust Fund.
beneficiaries: Public-use airports, air travelers, general aviation community

name: National Railroad Passenger Corporation (Amtrak) Grants
description: Provides federal grants to Amtrak for operating and capital expenses of the national intercity passenger rail system. Supports approximately 500 stations across 46 states with over 30 million annual riders.
beneficiaries: Amtrak riders, communities served by intercity passenger rail

name: Operations and Administration, Maritime Administration
description: Funds MARAD's programs supporting the U.S. merchant marine fleet, including the Maritime Security Program, Ready Reserve Force, mariner training at the U.S. Merchant Marine Academy, and ship disposal. Ensures sealift capacity for national defense.
beneficiaries: U.S. merchant marine industry, national defense sealift

name: Scientific and Technical Research and Services, National Institute of Standards and Technology
description: Funds NIST's laboratory research programs in advanced manufacturing, cybersecurity, physical sciences, and engineering to develop measurement standards, technology, and practices. Supports U.S. innovation and industrial competitiveness.
beneficiaries: U.S. industry, standards community, technology sector

name: Science, NASA
description: Funds NASA's space and Earth science programs including planetary exploration, astrophysics, heliophysics, and Earth observation missions. Supports missions such as the James Webb Space Telescope, Mars rovers, and climate monitoring satellites.
beneficiaries: Scientific research community, general public, climate and weather monitoring

name: Exploration, NASA
description: Funds NASA's human spaceflight exploration programs including the Artemis lunar program, Space Launch System, Orion spacecraft, and human landing systems. Aims to return humans to the Moon and eventually send crews to Mars.
beneficiaries: Aerospace industry, scientific community, national space exploration goals

name: Space Operations, NASA
description: Funds NASA's ongoing human spaceflight operations including the International Space Station, commercial crew transportation, space communications networks, and launch services. Manages ISS partnerships with international space agencies.
beneficiaries: Astronauts, ISS international partners, commercial space industry

name: Research and Related Activities, National Science Foundation
description: Funds fundamental scientific research and education across all non-medical fields of science and engineering through competitive grants to universities and research institutions. Supports approximately 25% of all federally funded basic research at academic institutions.
beneficiaries: University researchers, graduate students, scientific community

name: Salaries and Expenses, Smithsonian Institution
description: Funds the operation of the world's largest museum, education, and research complex, including 21 museums, the National Zoo, and nine research centers. Most Smithsonian museums are free and located on the National Mall.
beneficiaries: Museum visitors, researchers, educators, general public

name: Salaries and Expenses, Library of Congress
description: Funds operations of the world's largest library, which holds over 170 million items and serves as the research library for Congress. Administers the U.S. Copyright Office and provides services to the blind and print disabled.
beneficiaries: Members of Congress, researchers, copyright holders, general public

name: Government Publishing Office (Revolving Fund)
description: Funds the production and distribution of official federal government publications including the Congressional Record, Federal Register, and U.S. Code. Operates as a revolving fund reimbursed by customer agencies.
beneficiaries: Congress, federal agencies, public accessing government documents

name: Salaries and Expenses, Congressional Budget Office
description: Funds CBO's nonpartisan analysis of budgetary and economic issues for Congress, including cost estimates of legislation, budget projections, and economic forecasts. Created by the Congressional Budget Act of 1974.
beneficiaries: Members of Congress, congressional committees, public policy process

name: Family Housing Construction, Army
description: Funds construction and improvement of on-post family housing for Army service members and their families. Largely privatized since 1996 under the Military Housing Privatization Initiative.
beneficiaries: Army service members and families in on-post housing

name: Family Housing Operation and Maintenance, Army
description: Funds operation, maintenance, and utilities for Army family housing units not yet privatized. Covers housing management, furnishings, and leasing of family housing in areas without adequate private sector options.
beneficiaries: Army service members and families in government-managed housing

name: Family Housing Construction, Navy and Marine Corps
description: Funds construction, improvement, and planning of family housing for Navy and Marine Corps personnel at installations in the U.S. and overseas. Largely transitioned to private-public ventures under housing privatization.
beneficiaries: Navy and Marine Corps service members and families

name: Family Housing Construction, Air Force
description: Funds construction and improvement of Air Force family housing at domestic and overseas installations. Most stateside housing has been privatized; appropriation primarily supports overseas locations.
beneficiaries: Air Force service members and families

name: Defense Commissary Agency
description: Operates a worldwide chain of approximately 236 commissary stores on military installations providing groceries at cost plus a 5% surcharge to military families. Considered a key quality-of-life benefit for the military community.
beneficiaries: Active-duty military, retirees, reservists, and their families

name: Defense Logistics Agency Operations
description: Funds DLA's combat logistics support operations including supply chain management for fuel, food, clothing, construction materials, medical supplies, and spare parts for all military services. Manages over 5 million items.
beneficiaries: All branches of the U.S. military, federal agencies

name: General Fund, Defense Intelligence Agency
description: Funds DIA's military intelligence operations providing all-source intelligence analysis, human intelligence collection, and defense intelligence support to combatant commanders and policymakers. The primary producer of foreign military intelligence.
beneficiaries: Combatant commanders, Defense Department, national security policymakers

name: National Security Agency/Central Security Service
description: Funds NSA's signals intelligence collection and information assurance mission, providing foreign intelligence and cybersecurity capabilities. The largest U.S. intelligence agency by budget and personnel.
beneficiaries: Intelligence community, military operations, cybersecurity defense

name: Defense Contract Audit Agency
description: Funds DCAA's contract auditing services for the Department of Defense and other federal agencies, reviewing defense contractor pricing, costs, and financial systems. Performs approximately 5,000 audit reports annually.
beneficiaries: DoD acquisition community, taxpayers

name: Construction, Army Corps of Engineers, Civil Works
description: Funds the Army Corps of Engineers' civil works construction program for flood and storm damage reduction, navigation, ecosystem restoration, and water supply projects. Includes major levee, dam, and waterway infrastructure.
beneficiaries: Communities in flood-prone areas, commercial navigation, water supply users

name: Buildings and Facilities, Federal Prison System
description: Funds the construction, modernization, and repair of federal correctional institutions operated by the Bureau of Prisons. Supports capacity for the federal inmate population across all security levels.
beneficiaries: Federal prison system, correctional staff, public safety

name: Salaries and Expenses, Courts of Appeals, District Courts, and Other Judicial Services
description: Funds the operating costs of the federal court system including judges' staff, clerks of court, probation and pretrial services, court interpreters, and juror fees. Supports approximately 870 authorized federal judgeships.
beneficiaries: Federal judiciary, litigants, jurors, justice system

name: United States Tax Court
description: Funds operations of the independent judicial body where taxpayers can dispute IRS determinations of tax deficiencies before paying the contested amount. Handles approximately 30,000 cases annually.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers disputing IRS assessments

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Trade Commission
description: Funds the FTC's consumer protection and antitrust enforcement activities, including investigating unfair business practices, merger review, and consumer education. Funded primarily through Hart-Scott-Rodino merger filing fees.
beneficiaries: Consumers, competitive markets

name: Salaries and Expenses, Securities and Exchange Commission
description: Funds SEC's regulation of securities markets, including corporate disclosure oversight, enforcement against fraud, registration of broker-dealers, and investor protection. Funded through transaction fees and filing fees.
beneficiaries: Investors, securities markets, public companies

name: Commodity Futures Trading Commission
description: Funds regulation of U.S. commodity futures and options markets and the swaps market, including enforcement against market manipulation and fraud. Oversees designated contract markets and swap execution facilities.
beneficiaries: Market participants, agricultural producers, financial market integrity

name: Nuclear Regulatory Commission
description: Funds NRC's regulation of civilian nuclear power plants, research reactors, nuclear materials, and radioactive waste to protect public health and safety. Licenses and inspects approximately 93 operating commercial nuclear reactors.
beneficiaries: Communities near nuclear facilities, nuclear industry, public safety

name: Board of Governors, Federal Reserve System
description: Funds the Federal Reserve Board's operations including monetary policy, banking supervision, financial stability oversight, and payment system regulation. Self-funded through assessments on Federal Reserve Banks, not congressional appropriations.
beneficiaries: U.S. economy, banking system, monetary policy

name: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
description: Insures deposits up to $250,000 per depositor at member banks and thrift institutions, and resolves failed insured depository institutions. Funded by insurance premiums from member institutions, not taxpayer funds.
beneficiaries: Bank depositors, stability of the banking system

name: National Credit Union Administration
description: Charters, regulates, and insures federal credit unions and insures most state-chartered credit unions through the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund. Insures deposits up to $250,000 per member.
beneficiaries: Credit union members, credit union system

name: Farm Credit System Insurance Corporation
description: Insures the timely payment of principal and interest on obligations issued by Farm Credit System banks, maintaining confidence in the agricultural credit system. Funded through insurance premiums from FCS banks.
beneficiaries: Investors in Farm Credit System securities, agricultural borrowers

name: Government National Mortgage Association (Ginnie Mae)
description: Guarantees the timely payment of principal and interest on mortgage-backed securities backed by federally insured or guaranteed loans (FHA, VA, USDA). Provides liquidity to the government-insured mortgage market without taxpayer appropriations.
beneficiaries: Mortgage borrowers with government-insured loans, MBS investors

name: Federal Housing Finance Agency (Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Oversight)
description: Regulates and supervises Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and the Federal Home Loan Banks to ensure they operate safely and support the housing finance market. Placed Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac into conservatorship in September 2008.
beneficiaries: Mortgage market, homeowners, housing finance stability

name: Federal Home Loan Banks
description: A system of 11 government-sponsored enterprise banks that provide low-cost funding (advances) to member financial institutions to support housing finance, community investment, and liquidity. Privately capitalized by member institutions.
beneficiaries: Member banks, thrifts, credit unions, and insurance companies; homebuyers

name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (Bureau Fund)
description: Funds the CFPB's regulation and enforcement of federal consumer financial laws covering mortgages, credit cards, student loans, and other consumer financial products. Funded through Federal Reserve System transfers, not congressional appropriations.
beneficiaries: Consumers of financial products and services

name: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
description: Charters, regulates, and supervises national banks and federal savings associations to ensure they operate safely and provide fair access to financial services. Self-funded through assessments on supervised institutions.
beneficiaries: Customers of national banks, banking system stability

name: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN)
description: Safeguards the financial system from illicit use by collecting and analyzing financial intelligence data, primarily Bank Secrecy Act reports, and administering anti-money laundering regulations. Designated as the U.S. financial intelligence unit.
beneficiaries: Law enforcement, financial institutions, financial system integrity

name: Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC), Treasury
description: Administers and enforces U.S. economic sanctions programs against targeted foreign countries, terrorists, narcotics traffickers, and proliferators of weapons of mass destruction. Maintains the Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list.
beneficiaries: U.S. national security, international sanctions regime

name: Alcohol and Tobacco Tax and Trade Bureau (TTB)
description: Collects federal excise taxes on alcohol, tobacco, firearms, and ammunition, and regulates the alcohol and tobacco industries to protect consumers. Split from ATF by the Homeland Security Act of 2002.
beneficiaries: Federal revenue, consumers of alcohol and tobacco products

name: United States Mint, Public Enterprise Fund
description: Manufactures and distributes circulating coinage, bullion, and numismatic products for the nation. Operates as a public enterprise fund with revenues from seigniorage and sales offsetting production costs.
beneficiaries: General public, coin collectors, commerce

name: Bureau of Engraving and Printing, Operating Fund
description: Designs, manufactures, and supplies Federal Reserve notes (paper currency) and other security documents for the federal government. Operates as a revolving fund reimbursed by the Federal Reserve for currency production.
beneficiaries: Federal Reserve System, general public, commerce

name: Operations Support, Internal Revenue Service
description: Funds IRS core operational functions including rent, communications, IT infrastructure, security, printing, and postage needed to administer the tax system. Supports the foundational infrastructure for all IRS activities.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers, federal tax administration

name: Enforcement, Internal Revenue Service
description: Funds IRS examination of tax returns, collection of delinquent taxes, criminal investigation of tax fraud, and legal counsel activities. Historically returns significantly more revenue than its cost through compliance enforcement.
beneficiaries: Federal revenue, tax compliance, honest taxpayers

name: Business Systems Modernization, Internal Revenue Service
description: Funds IRS technology modernization efforts to replace legacy systems, improve e-filing capabilities, and enhance taxpayer services through digital transformation. Received a major boost from the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers, IRS operational efficiency

name: Customs and Border Protection, Duties, Taxes, and Fees
description: Accounts for the collection of customs duties, excise taxes, and user fees on imported goods entering the United States. One of the oldest sources of federal revenue, collected at ports of entry.
beneficiaries: Federal revenue, domestic industries under trade law protection

name: Immigration Examinations Fee Account, USCIS
description: Funds U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' adjudication of immigration benefit applications including naturalization, green cards, work permits, and asylum cases. Operates almost entirely on application fees rather than appropriations.
beneficiaries: Immigration applicants, employers of foreign workers

name: Indian Health Service, Services Account
description: Funds direct health care services to approximately 2.6 million American Indians and Alaska Natives through a network of hospitals, clinics, and health programs. Fulfills the federal trust responsibility for tribal health care.
beneficiaries: American Indians and Alaska Natives in federally recognized tribes

name: Immunization and Respiratory Diseases, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
description: Funds CDC's immunization programs including the Vaccines for Children program, pandemic influenza preparedness, and surveillance of vaccine-preventable diseases. Supports vaccine distribution infrastructure and outbreak response.
beneficiaries: Children, adults receiving vaccinations, public health

name: HIV/AIDS, Viral Hepatitis, STI, and TB Prevention, CDC
description: Funds CDC's domestic prevention programs for HIV/AIDS, sexually transmitted infections, viral hepatitis, and tuberculosis including surveillance, testing, and prevention interventions. Supports Ryan White-related surveillance and partner notification.
beneficiaries: Populations at risk for HIV, STIs, hepatitis, and TB

name: Chronic Disease Prevention and Health Promotion, CDC
description: Funds CDC programs addressing heart disease, cancer, diabetes, tobacco use, obesity, and other chronic conditions through surveillance, research, and community prevention grants. Chronic diseases account for the majority of U.S. deaths and health care costs.
beneficiaries: General public, populations at risk for chronic diseases

name: Salaries and Expenses, Food and Drug Administration
description: Funds FDA's regulation of food safety, pharmaceuticals, medical devices, biologics, cosmetics, tobacco, and veterinary products to protect public health. Funded through a combination of appropriations and industry user fees.
beneficiaries: Consumers, patients, food and pharmaceutical industries

name: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA)
