description: Self-financing federal power marketing administration that markets wholesale electricity from 31 federal dams and one nuclear plant in the Pacific Northwest, and operates 15,000 miles of transmission lines. Created by the Bonneville Project Act of 1937.
beneficiaries: Pacific Northwest utilities, industrial customers, ratepayers; fish and wildlife through mitigation programs

name: Tennessee Valley Authority
description: Federally owned corporation providing electricity, flood control, navigation, and economic development in the Tennessee Valley region across seven states. Created as a New Deal agency; now self-financing through power sales, no longer receiving appropriations.
beneficiaries: Approximately 10 million people in the Tennessee Valley region; local power companies; regional economy

name: Railroad Retirement Board Benefits
description: Pays retirement, survivor, disability, unemployment, and sickness benefits to railroad workers and their families under a system separate from Social Security. Funded by payroll taxes on railroad employers and employees.
beneficiaries: Retired and disabled railroad workers, their spouses, survivors, and unemployed/sick railroad workers

name: Foreign Military Financing, State
description: Provides grants and loans to foreign governments for purchase of U.S. defense equipment, services, and training to advance U.S. national security interests. The largest share historically goes to Israel and Egypt.
beneficiaries: Allied and partner foreign governments; U.S. defense industry; U.S. national security interests

name: Economic Support Fund, State/USAID
description: Flexible foreign assistance account supporting U.S. economic and political interests abroad through budget support, development assistance, and stabilization programs in strategically important countries. Managed jointly by State and USAID.
beneficiaries: Foreign governments and populations in strategically important countries; U.S. foreign policy interests

name: USAID Operating Expenses
description: Administrative account funding USAID staff salaries, overseas mission operations, travel, and infrastructure needed to manage U.S. foreign development and humanitarian assistance programs worldwide.
beneficiaries: USAID workforce; indirectly, populations in developing countries receiving U.S. assistance

name: Export-Import Bank
description: Self-sustaining federal agency providing export credit insurance, loan guarantees, and direct loans to support U.S. exports when private financing is unavailable, countering foreign export credit competition. Operates at no net cost to taxpayers in most years.
beneficiaries: U.S. exporters (especially small businesses), their foreign buyers, and U.S. workers in export industries

name: SBA Disaster Loans
description: Provides low-interest, long-term federal disaster loans to homeowners, renters, businesses, and nonprofits to repair or replace disaster-damaged property. The primary federal disaster loan program and SBA's largest program by dollar volume.
beneficiaries: Homeowners, renters, businesses, and nonprofits in presidentially declared disaster areas

name: Department of Health and Human Services
description: Cabinet-level department responsible for protecting the health of Americans and providing essential human services. Encompasses Medicare, Medicaid, NIH research, FDA, CDC, and social service programs.
beneficiaries: All Americans, particularly elderly, low-income, disabled, and children

name: Department of the Treasury
description: Manages federal finances including revenue collection (IRS), debt servicing, currency production, and economic policy. Handles interest payments on national debt, tax refunds, and financial system oversight.
beneficiaries: Federal government operations, taxpayers receiving refunds, holders of U.S. debt

name: Social Security Administration
description: Independent agency administering Social Security retirement, survivors, and disability insurance programs funded through payroll taxes. Provides monthly cash benefits to eligible retirees, disabled workers, and survivors of deceased workers.
beneficiaries: Retired workers, disabled individuals, survivors and dependents of deceased or retired workers

name: Department of Defense
description: Cabinet-level department providing military forces to deter war and protect national security. Funds the Army, Navy, Air Force, Marine Corps, Space Force, and defense agencies including personnel, operations, procurement, and R&D.
beneficiaries: U.S. military servicemembers, national security infrastructure, defense contractors, allied nations

name: Federal Old-Age and Survivors Insurance Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, Social Security Administration
description: Trust fund that receives Social Security payroll tax revenues and pays retirement and survivors benefits. Holds dedicated receipts from FICA/SECA taxes and interest on Treasury securities to fund monthly benefit payments.
beneficiaries: Retired workers aged 62+, spouses, children, and survivors of deceased insured workers

name: Interest on the Public Debt (Indefinite), Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Indefinite appropriation to pay interest owed on Treasury securities including bills, notes, bonds, and savings bonds held by the public and government accounts. This is the federal government's cost of borrowing.
beneficiaries: Holders of U.S. Treasury securities including domestic and foreign investors, mutual funds, pension funds, and foreign governments

name: Grants to States for Medicaid, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Health and Human Services
description: Federal matching grants to states for the Medicaid program, providing health coverage to low-income individuals, families, pregnant women, elderly, and people with disabilities. The federal share (FMAP) varies by state.
beneficiaries: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and disabled individuals enrolled in state Medicaid programs

name: Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, Health and Human Services
description: Trust fund financing Medicare Part B (physician/outpatient services) and Part D (prescription drugs). Funded by beneficiary premiums (~25%) and general revenue transfers (~75%).
beneficiaries: Medicare-eligible individuals aged 65+ and certain disabled persons receiving Part B and Part D benefits

name: Payments to Health Care Trust Funds, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Health and Human Services
description: General fund transfers to the Medicare trust funds representing the government's employer share and the general revenue subsidy for SMI (Parts B and D). These intragovernmental transfers supplement payroll tax and premium revenue.
beneficiaries: Medicare trust funds (HI and SMI) and indirectly all Medicare beneficiaries

name: Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, Health and Human Services
description: Trust fund financing Medicare Part A, which covers inpatient hospital care, skilled nursing facility care, hospice, and home health services. Primarily funded by the 2.9% HI payroll tax.
beneficiaries: Medicare Part A beneficiaries including elderly aged 65+ and eligible disabled individuals needing hospital and post-acute care

name: Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Cabinet-level department providing healthcare, disability compensation, education benefits, home loan guarantees, and burial services to military veterans. Operates the nation's largest integrated healthcare system.
beneficiaries: U.S. military veterans, their dependents, and survivors

name: Refunding Internal Revenue Collections (Indefinite), Treasury
description: Indefinite appropriation for IRS tax refunds to taxpayers who overpaid their federal income, employment, estate, or excise taxes. Covers individual and corporate refunds including refundable credit payments.
beneficiaries: Individual and corporate taxpayers who overpaid federal taxes

name: Department of Agriculture
description: Cabinet-level department overseeing farming policy, food safety, nutrition assistance (SNAP, school meals), rural development, forestry, and agricultural research. Administers the nation's largest nutrition safety-net programs.
beneficiaries: Farmers, ranchers, low-income households receiving food assistance, rural communities, consumers

name: Office of Personnel Management
description: Independent agency managing the federal civilian workforce, including the Civil Service Retirement System, Federal Employees Retirement System, and Federal Employees Health Benefits program. Most of its budget flows through retirement and health benefit trust funds.
beneficiaries: Current and retired federal civilian employees and their families

name: Department of Education
description: Cabinet-level department administering federal student financial aid (Pell Grants, student loans), K-12 education grants (Title I), special education funding (IDEA), and education research. Does not operate schools directly.
beneficiaries: K-12 students, college students, schools, teachers, state and local education agencies

name: Compensation and Pensions, Veterans Benefits Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds disability compensation for service-connected conditions, pensions for wartime veterans with limited income, and dependency and indemnity compensation (DIC) for survivors of veterans who died from service-related causes.
beneficiaries: Disabled veterans, low-income wartime veterans, surviving spouses and dependents of deceased veterans

name: Federal Disability Insurance Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, Social Security Administration
description: Trust fund receiving dedicated payroll tax revenue to pay Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI) benefits to workers who become severely disabled before retirement age, and to their eligible dependents.
beneficiaries: Disabled workers under full retirement age, their spouses, and dependent children

name: Defense Working Capital Fund, Defense
description: Revolving fund operating on a break-even basis to finance commercial-type activities within the Department of Defense such as depot maintenance, supply management, transportation, and industrial operations. Customers (military services) pay for goods and services received.
beneficiaries: DoD components, military services, and defense agencies requiring logistics and support services

name: Department of Transportation
description: Cabinet-level department overseeing federal highway, aviation, rail, transit, maritime, and pipeline transportation programs. Funds infrastructure construction, safety regulation, and transportation research.
beneficiaries: State and local transportation agencies, highway and transit users, airlines, railroads, the traveling public

name: Department of Homeland Security
description: Cabinet-level department consolidating border security (CBP), immigration enforcement (ICE), cybersecurity (CISA), disaster response (FEMA), transportation security (TSA), Coast Guard, and Secret Service. Created after the September 11, 2001 attacks.
beneficiaries: General public, border communities, disaster-affected populations, critical infrastructure operators

name: Refundable Premium Assistance Tax Credit, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds the Affordable Care Act's premium tax credits (subsidies) for individuals and families purchasing health insurance through the Health Insurance Marketplace. The refundable portion is paid to insurers on behalf of enrollees.
beneficiaries: Individuals and families with incomes between 100-400% of the federal poverty level purchasing ACA marketplace health plans

name: Medicare Prescription Drug Account, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Health and Human Services
description: Account within the SMI Trust Fund financing Medicare Part D prescription drug benefits, established by the Medicare Modernization Act of 2003. Pays subsidies to private prescription drug plans and provides the low-income subsidy.
beneficiaries: Medicare beneficiaries enrolled in Part D prescription drug plans, particularly low-income enrollees receiving extra help

name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program, Food and Nutrition Service, Agriculture
description: The nation's largest domestic food assistance program (formerly Food Stamps), providing electronic benefit transfer (EBT) cards to low-income households to purchase food. Benefits are based on household size, income, and expenses.
beneficiaries: Low-income individuals and families meeting income and asset eligibility requirements

name: Civil Service Retirement and Disability Fund, Office of Personnel Management
description: Trust fund paying retirement annuities, survivor benefits, and disability benefits to federal civilian employees and retirees under CSRS and FERS. Funded by employee and agency contributions plus general fund payments for the unfunded liability.
beneficiaries: Retired federal civilian employees, disabled federal employees, and their survivors

name: Department of Housing and Urban Development
description: Cabinet-level department administering federal housing programs including rental assistance (Section 8), public housing, FHA mortgage insurance, community development block grants, and homelessness assistance. Works to increase homeownership and affordable housing.
beneficiaries: Low-income renters, homebuyers, public housing residents, homeless individuals, communities receiving development grants

name: Department of Energy
description: Cabinet-level department responsible for nuclear weapons stewardship (NNSA), energy research and development, national laboratories, nuclear waste cleanup, and energy policy. Also manages the Strategic Petroleum Reserve and Power Marketing Administrations.
beneficiaries: National security apparatus, energy researchers, national laboratory scientists, energy consumers, communities affected by nuclear legacy sites

name: Deposit Insurance Fund, Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
description: Insurance fund protecting depositors at FDIC-insured banks up to $250,000 per depositor per institution. Funded by premiums assessed on insured banks, not taxpayer dollars, and used to resolve failed banks and pay insured depositors.
beneficiaries: Depositors at FDIC-insured banks and savings institutions

name: Medical Services, Veterans Health Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds inpatient and outpatient medical care, mental health services, substance abuse treatment, and long-term care for eligible veterans through the VA healthcare system. Operates approximately 170 VA medical centers and 1,100+ outpatient clinics.
beneficiaries: Enrolled veterans receiving healthcare through the VA medical system

name: Defense Working Capital Fund, Defense Agencies
description: Revolving fund sub-account for defense agencies providing logistics, supply chain management, and support services on a reimbursable basis. Includes the Defense Logistics Agency and other centralized defense support activities.
beneficiaries: Defense agencies and military departments purchasing centralized support services

name: Employees' Health Benefits Fund, Office of Personnel Management
description: Trust fund for the Federal Employees Health Benefits (FEHB) program, the largest employer-sponsored health insurance program in the U.S. Pools government and employee premium contributions to pay health insurance carriers for enrolled federal workers and retirees.
beneficiaries: Active federal employees, retirees, and their dependents enrolled in FEHB health plans

name: Supplemental Security Income Program, Social Security Administration
description: Federally-funded means-tested program providing monthly cash payments to aged, blind, and disabled individuals with limited income and resources. Unlike Social Security, SSI is funded from general revenues, not payroll taxes.
beneficiaries: Aged (65+), blind, and disabled individuals with limited income and resources, including children with disabilities

name: Department of Labor
description: Cabinet-level department overseeing workplace safety (OSHA), unemployment insurance, job training, wage and hour standards, workers' compensation, and labor statistics. Administers federal unemployment trust fund and workforce development programs.
beneficiaries: Workers, unemployed individuals, job seekers, employers, labor unions

name: Operation and Maintenance, Defense-Wide, Defense
description: Funds day-to-day operating costs for defense agencies and activities not assigned to a specific military service, including SOCOM, DISA, DARPA, missile defense, and joint operations. Covers civilian pay, supplies, equipment maintenance, and contracted services.
beneficiaries: Defense agencies, combatant commands, special operations forces, and defense-wide support organizations

name: Payment Where Earned Income Credit Exceeds Liability for Tax, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds the refundable portion of the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC), the outlay that occurs when the credit amount exceeds the taxpayer's income tax liability. The EITC incentivizes and supplements earnings for low-to-moderate income working families.
beneficiaries: Low-to-moderate income working individuals and families, particularly those with qualifying children

name: Federal-Aid Highways, Liquidation of Contract Authorization, Federal Highway Administration, Transportation
description: Provides cash to liquidate obligations incurred under the Federal-Aid Highway program's contract authority, primarily from the Highway Trust Fund. Funds construction, reconstruction, and improvement of the National Highway System and other federal-aid roads.
beneficiaries: State departments of transportation, highway users, motorists, and communities benefiting from road infrastructure

name: Department of State
description: Cabinet-level department conducting U.S. foreign policy and diplomacy, operating embassies and consulates worldwide, issuing passports and visas, and administering foreign assistance programs. Also manages international organizations contributions.
beneficiaries: U.S. citizens abroad, foreign policy establishment, international partners, foreign aid recipients

name: Environmental Protection Agency
description: Independent agency protecting human health and the environment through regulation and enforcement of clean air, clean water, toxic substances, and waste management laws. Also funds Superfund cleanup, water infrastructure grants, and environmental research.
beneficiaries: General public, communities with pollution or contaminated sites, state environmental agencies, water and wastewater utilities

name: Payments to Social Security Trust Funds, Social Security Administration
description: General fund transfers to the OASI and DI trust funds to reimburse for special payments such as gratuitous military wage credits, unnegotiated checks, and the taxation of Social Security benefits. Ensures trust funds are held harmless for policy decisions.
beneficiaries: Social Security trust funds (OASI and DI), indirectly all Social Security beneficiaries

name: Payment to Civil Service Retirement and Disability Fund, Office of Personnel Management
description: Annual general fund transfer to the CSRDF to amortize the unfunded liability of the Civil Service Retirement System and cover the government's share as employer. This intragovernmental payment ensures the retirement fund remains actuarially sound.
beneficiaries: Civil Service Retirement and Disability Fund, indirectly retired and active federal civilian employees

name: Department of Justice
description: Cabinet-level department enforcing federal law and administering justice, including the FBI, DEA, ATF, U.S. Marshals, Bureau of Prisons, and U.S. Attorneys. Also oversees immigration courts, civil rights enforcement, and antitrust litigation.
beneficiaries: General public, law enforcement communities, federal inmates, crime victims, state and local justice agencies

name: Research, Development, Test, and Evaluation, Air Force
description: Funds Air Force research, technology development, prototyping, and testing of aircraft, weapons systems, space systems, and advanced technologies. Covers basic/applied research through operational system development and evaluation.
beneficiaries: U.S. Air Force, defense contractors, research institutions, national security

name: Department of Commerce
description: Cabinet-level department promoting economic growth through trade, technology, weather forecasting (NOAA), census, patent/trademark protection (USPTO), broadband deployment (NTIA), and standards (NIST). Recently received large broadband infrastructure funding.
beneficiaries: Businesses, exporters, inventors, coastal communities, weather-dependent industries, broadband-underserved communities

name: Department of the Interior
description: Cabinet-level department managing federal lands, national parks, wildlife refuges, Bureau of Reclamation water projects, geological surveys (USGS), and programs for Native American and Alaska Native communities (BIA). Oversees approximately 500 million acres of federal land.
beneficiaries: National park visitors, Native American tribes, western water users, wildlife, energy and mineral lessees on federal lands

name: General Services Administration
description: Independent agency managing federal real estate, procurement, and technology services. Operates the Federal Buildings Fund, provides government-wide acquisition contracts, manages the federal vehicle fleet, and runs the Technology Transformation Services.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies purchasing goods, services, office space, vehicles, and technology solutions

name: Student Financial Assistance, Education
description: Funds federal Pell Grants, Federal Supplemental Educational Opportunity Grants (FSEOG), and Federal Work-Study programs for undergraduate students demonstrating financial need. Pell Grants are the largest component.
beneficiaries: Low-income undergraduate students attending colleges and universities

name: Defense Working Capital Fund, Navy
description: Revolving fund sub-account for Navy-managed working capital activities including naval shipyards, supply operations, transportation, and depot maintenance. Operates on a break-even cost-recovery basis from customer orders.
beneficiaries: U.S. Navy and Marine Corps units purchasing maintenance, supply, and industrial services

name: Taxation on OASDI Benefits, HI, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Health and Human Services
description: Transfers income taxes collected on Social Security benefits to the Medicare Hospital Insurance Trust Fund. Established by the 1993 legislation that directed revenue from taxing up to 85% of OASDI benefits to the HI trust fund.
beneficiaries: Medicare Hospital Insurance Trust Fund, indirectly Medicare Part A beneficiaries

name: Disaster Relief Fund, Federal Emergency Management Agency, Homeland Security
description: Primary fund for federal disaster response and recovery under the Stafford Act, providing individual assistance (temporary housing, grants), public assistance (infrastructure repair), and hazard mitigation grants following presidential disaster declarations.
beneficiaries: Disaster-affected individuals, households, state/local/tribal governments, and communities recovering from federally declared disasters

name: Research, Development, Test, and Evaluation, Defense-Wide
description: Funds R&D for defense agencies not assigned to a specific military service, including DARPA, the Missile Defense Agency, DTRA, and other defense-wide technology programs. Covers advanced research, prototyping, and testing of cross-cutting defense capabilities.
beneficiaries: Defense agencies, DARPA researchers, defense contractors, national security innovation base

name: Small Business Administration
description: Independent agency providing loan guarantees, disaster loans, small business counseling, contracting assistance, and venture capital support to American small businesses. Also administers the Paycheck Protection Program and disaster lending.
beneficiaries: Small business owners, entrepreneurs, disaster-affected businesses and homeowners

name: Child Nutrition Programs, Food and Nutrition Service
description: Funds the National School Lunch Program, School Breakfast Program, Child and Adult Care Food Program, Summer Food Service Program, and Special Milk Program. Provides meals and snacks to children in schools, childcare, and summer programs.
beneficiaries: Children in schools and childcare facilities, particularly those from low-income families receiving free or reduced-price meals

name: Corps of Engineers - Civil Works
description: Funds the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers' civil works mission including flood risk management, navigation (locks, dams, harbors), ecosystem restoration, hydropower, water supply, and recreation. Plans, designs, builds, and maintains water resources infrastructure.
beneficiaries: Flood-prone communities, inland waterway shippers, port users, hydropower consumers, recreational users of Corps projects

name: Tenant-Based Rental Assistance, Public and Indian Housing, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds the Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) program, the largest federal rental assistance program. Provides portable vouchers to low-income families to rent housing in the private market, paying the difference between 30% of income and fair market rent.
beneficiaries: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals using Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers

name: Federal Communications Commission
description: Independent regulatory commission overseeing interstate and international communications by radio, television, wire, satellite, and cable. Administers spectrum auctions, Universal Service Fund, broadband regulation, and media ownership rules.
beneficiaries: Telecommunications consumers, broadcasters, wireless carriers, rural and low-income households receiving Universal Service subsidies

name: Medical Community Care, Veterans Health Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds healthcare purchased from private-sector community providers for eligible veterans under the VA MISSION Act, when VA facilities cannot provide timely or geographically accessible care. Replaced the former Veterans Choice Program.
beneficiaries: Veterans receiving VA-authorized care from private-sector community healthcare providers

name: Acquisition Services Fund, General Services Administration
description: Revolving fund providing government-wide procurement, IT, and professional services to federal agencies on a fee-for-service basis. Includes the Federal Acquisition Service's supply chain, fleet management, telecommunications, and assisted acquisition services.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies purchasing goods, IT solutions, fleet vehicles, and acquisition support through GSA

name: Research, Development, Test, and Evaluation, Navy
description: Funds Navy and Marine Corps research, development, testing, and evaluation of ships, submarines, aircraft, weapons, electronics, and marine technologies. Includes the Office of Naval Research and Naval Research Laboratory programs.
beneficiaries: U.S. Navy and Marine Corps, naval research laboratories, defense contractors, maritime security

name: Defense Working Capital Fund, Air Force
description: Revolving fund sub-account for Air Force working capital activities including depot maintenance (air logistics complexes), supply management, and transportation. Operates on a cost-recovery basis from Air Force and other DoD customer orders.
beneficiaries: U.S. Air Force units purchasing depot maintenance, spare parts, and logistics services

name: National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: Independent agency conducting space exploration, aeronautics research, Earth science, and technology development. Operates human spaceflight programs (ISS, Artemis), robotic missions, and maintains research centers across the country.
beneficiaries: Scientific community, astronauts, aerospace industry, general public benefiting from space technology spinoffs

name: Payment Where Child Tax Credit Exceeds Liability for Tax, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds the refundable portion (Additional Child Tax Credit) of the Child Tax Credit, where the credit exceeds the taxpayer's income tax liability and the excess is paid as a refund. Supports families with qualifying children.
beneficiaries: Low-to-moderate income families with qualifying children under age 17

name: Agency for International Development
description: Independent agency (USAID) administering U.S. foreign economic and humanitarian assistance to developing countries. Funds programs in global health, food security, democracy promotion, economic growth, education, and disaster relief worldwide.
beneficiaries: Developing country populations, NGOs implementing USAID programs, partner governments, humanitarian crisis-affected populations

name: Universal Service Fund, Federal Communications Commission
description: Fund administered by USAC providing subsidies for telecommunications and broadband in high-cost/rural areas, low-income households (Lifeline), schools and libraries (E-Rate), and rural healthcare facilities. Funded by assessments on telecom carriers.
beneficiaries: Rural telecommunications users, low-income households, schools, libraries, rural healthcare providers

name: Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment Program, National Telecommunications and Information Administration, Commerce
description: Grant program established by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (2021) to deploy broadband infrastructure to unserved and underserved areas. Distributes formula-based grants to states, territories, and DC for broadband expansion.
beneficiaries: Unserved and underserved communities lacking reliable high-speed internet, state broadband offices

name: Weapons Activities, National Nuclear Security Administration, Energy
description: Funds the stewardship, maintenance, and modernization of the U.S. nuclear weapons stockpile without underground testing. Covers warhead life extension programs, stockpile surveillance, weapons dismantlement, and the nuclear weapons production complex.
beneficiaries: NNSA weapons laboratories (Los Alamos, Sandia, Lawrence Livermore), nuclear weapons production plants, national security

name: Federal Crop Insurance Corporation Fund, Risk Management Agency, Agriculture
description: Funds the federal crop insurance program that subsidizes premiums and reimburses private insurers for crop and revenue losses. Provides risk management tools to farmers for losses due to weather, disease, price declines, and other perils.
beneficiaries: Farmers and ranchers purchasing federally subsidized crop insurance, approved insurance providers (AIPs)

name: Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation
description: Federal corporation insuring private-sector defined benefit pension plans. When a company's pension plan fails, PBGC pays guaranteed benefits to retirees and workers up to statutory limits. Funded by premiums from insured plans, not taxpayer dollars.
beneficiaries: Workers and retirees in failed private-sector defined benefit pension plans

name: Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation Fund, Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation, Labor
description: The operating fund of PBGC that receives premiums and pays benefits. Same entity as #89 but shown as the specific fund account within the Department of Labor organizational structure.
beneficiaries: Workers and retirees in failed private-sector defined benefit pension plans

name: Operations and Support, U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Homeland Security
description: Funds CBP's primary mission of securing U.S. borders at and between ports of entry, including Border Patrol agents, customs officers, agricultural inspectors, and trade enforcement. Covers personnel compensation, technology, and operational costs.
beneficiaries: U.S. border communities, legitimate travelers and traders, CBP personnel

name: State Children's Health Insurance Fund, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Health and Human Services
description: Funds the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP), providing federal matching funds to states for health coverage of children in families with incomes too high for Medicaid but too low to afford private insurance. Some states also cover pregnant women.
beneficiaries: Uninsured children in low-to-moderate income families, and in some states, pregnant women

name: Research, Development, Test, and Evaluation, Space Force, Air Force
description: Funds R&D for Space Force capabilities including space launch, satellite systems, space domain awareness, and space-based missile warning. Established when the Space Force was created as a separate service branch in 2019.
beneficiaries: U.S. Space Force, space industry contractors, national security space operations

name: Concurrent Receipt Accrual Payments to the Military Retirement Fund, Defense
description: Funds the Treasury's contribution for concurrent receipt of military retirement pay and VA disability compensation (Combat-Related Special Compensation and Concurrent Retirement and Disability Pay). Eliminated the offset that previously reduced military retired pay dollar-for-dollar for VA disability payments.
beneficiaries: Military retirees with service-connected disabilities who receive both military retirement pay and VA disability compensation

name: Cost of War Toxic Exposures Fund, Departmental Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Mandatory funding established by the PACT Act of 2022 to cover healthcare and benefits costs for veterans exposed to toxic substances (burn pits, Agent Orange, radiation) during military service. Ensures expanded eligibility does not strain VA's discretionary budget.
beneficiaries: Veterans exposed to burn pits, Agent Orange, radiation, and other toxic substances during military service

name: Railroad Retirement Board
description: Independent agency administering retirement, survivor, disability, unemployment, and sickness benefits for railroad workers and their families. Functions as a separate Social Security-like system for the railroad industry, funded by rail employer and employee payroll taxes.
beneficiaries: Retired, disabled, and unemployed railroad workers and their spouses, survivors, and dependents

name: Temporary Assistance for Needy Families, Administration for Children and Families, Health and Human Services
description: Block grant to states providing cash assistance, work support, childcare, and other services to low-income families with children. Replaced the former Aid to Families with Dependent Children (AFDC) program under the 1996 welfare reform law, with work requirements and time limits.
beneficiaries: Low-income families with children, particularly single-parent households meeting work requirements

name: Research, Development, Test, and Evaluation, Army
description: Funds Army research, development, testing, and evaluation of ground combat vehicles, weapons, soldier systems, aviation, missile defense, and communications technologies. Includes Army Research Laboratory, DEVCOM, and major weapons program development.
beneficiaries: U.S. Army, Army research laboratories, defense contractors, soldiers benefiting from improved equipment

name: Project-Based Rental Assistance, Housing Programs, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds Section 8 project-based rental assistance contracts with private landlords who set aside units for low-income tenants. Unlike tenant-based vouchers, the subsidy is attached to the specific housing unit, not the tenant.
beneficiaries: Low-income tenants in privately owned subsidized housing developments with project-based Section 8 contracts

name: Special Education, Education
description: Funds grants to states under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) to support special education and related services for children with disabilities ages 3-21. Includes Part B state grants and Part C early intervention for infants and toddlers.
beneficiaries: Children and youth with disabilities, state and local education agencies, early intervention programs

name: Children and Families Services Programs, Administration for Children and Families, Health and Human Services
description: Funds Head Start/Early Head Start, child welfare services, child abuse prevention, runaway and homeless youth programs, and community services. Head Start is the largest component, providing early childhood education to low-income children.
beneficiaries: Low-income preschool children (Head Start), at-risk youth, families receiving child welfare and community services

name: Government Payment for Annuitants, Employees' Health Benefits, Office of Personnel Management
description: Funds the government's (employer) share of health insurance premiums for federal retiree annuitants enrolled in the Federal Employees Health Benefits program. This payment parallels the employer contribution made for active employees.
beneficiaries: Retired federal civilian employees and their dependents enrolled in FEHB health insurance

name: Formula and Bus Grants, Liquidation of Contract Authorization, Federal Transit Administration, Transportation
description: Provides cash to liquidate obligations for formula-based transit capital and operating grants to states and local transit agencies from the Mass Transit Account of the Highway Trust Fund. Funds bus and rail transit capital investments, preventive maintenance, and operations in urbanized and rural areas.
beneficiaries: Public transit agencies, bus and rail riders, urbanized and rural communities with public transportation systems

name: Salaries and Expenses, Social Security Administration
description: Administrative budget of SSA covering personnel costs, IT systems, field office operations, and administrative expenses for processing Social Security and SSI claims, issuing Social Security numbers, and conducting disability determinations.
beneficiaries: SSA employees, indirectly all Social Security and SSI applicants and beneficiaries served by SSA operations

name: Readjustment Benefits, Veterans Benefits Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds education and training benefits for veterans including the Post-9/11 GI Bill, Montgomery GI Bill, Vocational Rehabilitation and Employment, and other readjustment programs helping veterans transition to civilian life. Covers tuition, housing allowances, and book stipends.
beneficiaries: Post-service veterans pursuing education or training, service members transitioning to civilian employment

name: Defense Working Capital Fund, Army
description: Revolving fund sub-account for Army working capital activities including depot maintenance (arsenals and depots), supply management, ordnance, and industrial operations. Operates on a cost-recovery basis from Army and other DoD customers.
beneficiaries: U.S. Army units purchasing depot maintenance, supply, and ammunition services

name: Operations, Federal Aviation Administration, Transportation
description: Funds FAA's air traffic control system operations, including controller salaries, facility maintenance, flight inspection, and aviation safety oversight. This is FAA's largest account, keeping the national airspace system running 24/7.
beneficiaries: Airlines, pilots, air travelers, the national airspace system, FAA air traffic controllers and safety inspectors

name: Revolving Fund, Corps of Engineers, Civil
description: Revolving fund for the Corps of Engineers' reimbursable civil works activities including work for other federal agencies, state/local governments, and foreign governments. Recovers costs from customers for engineering, construction, and environmental services.
beneficiaries: Federal, state, and local government agencies and foreign entities purchasing Corps engineering and construction services

name: Department of Defense, Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund
description: Trust fund financing TRICARE for Life benefits for Medicare-eligible military retirees and their dependents. Established by the National Defense Authorization Act for FY2001 to pre-fund the accruing health benefit liability for future retirees.
beneficiaries: Medicare-eligible military retirees aged 65+ and their dependents enrolled in TRICARE for Life

name: Operations, Airport and Airway Trust Fund, Federal Aviation Administration, Transportation
description: FAA operations funded from the Airport and Airway Trust Fund (financed by aviation excise taxes on tickets, fuel, and cargo). Covers air traffic control and aviation safety activities supported by dedicated aviation user fees and taxes.
beneficiaries: Airlines, general aviation, air travelers, the aviation system funded by aviation taxes

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Bureau of Investigation, Justice
description: Funds FBI operations including counterterrorism, counterintelligence, cybercrime, criminal investigations, and laboratory services. Covers agent and support personnel salaries, equipment, travel, and operational costs for the nation's primary federal law enforcement agency.
beneficiaries: FBI agents and staff, federal law enforcement operations, the general public protected by FBI investigations

name: Diplomatic and Consular Programs, State
description: Primary operating account for the State Department, funding salaries and operations at embassies and consulates worldwide, diplomatic security, passport and visa services, and policy formulation. Supports approximately 275 diplomatic posts globally.
beneficiaries: U.S. diplomats, Foreign Service officers, U.S. citizens abroad, visa applicants

name: Global Health Programs, State
description: Funds U.S. global health initiatives managed by the State Department, primarily PEPFAR (President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief), as well as programs addressing malaria, tuberculosis, maternal and child health, and global health security in developing countries.
beneficiaries: Populations in developing countries affected by HIV/AIDS, malaria, TB, and other health challenges

name: Medical Support and Compliance, Veterans Health Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds VHA's administrative, management, and support functions including financial management, human resources, IT, compliance oversight, and other infrastructure supporting the delivery of veteran healthcare services.
beneficiaries: VA healthcare system administration, indirectly veterans receiving VA medical care

name: Operations and Support, Coast Guard, Homeland Security
description: Funds Coast Guard operating expenses including maritime law enforcement, search and rescue, aids to navigation, marine safety, port security, and drug/migrant interdiction. Covers active-duty military and civilian personnel, vessel and aircraft operations.
beneficiaries: Maritime travelers, boaters, coastal communities, port facilities, Coast Guard personnel

name: Operations and Support, Transportation Security Administration, Homeland Security
description: Funds TSA's airport security screening operations including passenger and baggage screening, Federal Air Marshal Service, vetting programs, and surface transportation security. Covers transportation security officers (screeners) at approximately 440 airports.
beneficiaries: Air travelers, airlines, airports, the traveling public

name: Community Development Fund, Community Planning and Development, Housing and Urban Development
description: Funds the Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) program providing flexible formula grants to states, cities, and counties for housing, infrastructure, economic development, and public services benefiting low-and-moderate income communities.
beneficiaries: Low-and-moderate income communities, local governments, neighborhood organizations undertaking community development

name: Risk Adjustment Program Payments, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, Health and Human Services
description: ACA risk adjustment program that transfers funds from health insurance plans with lower-risk enrollees to plans with higher-risk enrollees in the individual and small group markets. Designed to stabilize premiums and reduce incentives for insurers to avoid sick enrollees.
beneficiaries: Health insurance issuers in ACA marketplaces, indirectly consumers in individual and small group health insurance markets

name: Commodity Credit Corporation Fund, Agriculture
description: Government-owned corporation fund providing price and income support to agricultural producers through loans, purchases, and payments. Also funds conservation programs, export promotion, and commodity donations for food assistance. Has permanent borrowing authority from the Treasury.
beneficiaries: Farmers and ranchers receiving commodity price supports, conservation payments, and export assistance

name: Credit Reform: Interest Paid on Uninvested Funds (Indefinite), Bureau of the Fiscal Service, Treasury
description: Indefinite appropriation under the Federal Credit Reform Act of 1990 covering interest costs on Treasury funds that have been disbursed for federal credit programs (loans and loan guarantees) but not yet offset by collections. Represents the government's borrowing cost for credit program cash flows.
beneficiaries: Federal credit program accounts (student loans, housing, SBA, etc.) requiring interest subsidy payments from Treasury

name: General Operating Expenses, Veterans Benefits Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Administrative account funding VBA's processing of disability compensation, pension, education, home loan, insurance, and burial benefit claims. Covers staffing, IT systems, and operational costs for adjudicating millions of veteran benefit claims annually.
beneficiaries: VBA employees, indirectly all veterans and dependents applying for VA benefits

name: Payments for Foster Care and Permanency, Administration for Children and Families, Health and Human Services
description: Federal matching payments to states for foster care maintenance (Title IV-E), adoption assistance, and kinship guardianship assistance. Reimburses states for room, board, and care of eligible children placed in foster family homes or childcare institutions.
beneficiaries: Children in foster care, adoptive families, kinship guardians, state child welfare agencies

name: Operations and Support, U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, Homeland Security
description: Funds ICE's immigration enforcement operations including detention and removal of undocumented immigrants, Homeland Security Investigations (transnational crime, human trafficking, cybercrime), and student/exchange visitor program oversight.
beneficiaries: ICE officers and agents, immigration enforcement operations, communities affected by transnational crime

name: National Science Foundation
description: Independent agency funding fundamental research and education across all non-medical fields of science and engineering. Awards grants to universities and research institutions, supports STEM education, and funds major research facilities and infrastructure.
beneficiaries: University researchers, graduate students, STEM educators, scientific community across all non-medical disciplines

name: Exchange Stabilization Fund, Office of the Secretary, Treasury
description: Fund managed by the Treasury Secretary to intervene in foreign exchange markets to stabilize the dollar's value, provide emergency loans or guarantees, and address international financial crises. Used notably to guarantee money market funds in 2008 and backstop pandemic lending facilities.
beneficiaries: U.S. and global financial system stability, the dollar's international standing

name: Payment to Department of Defense Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund
description: Annual accrual payment from the general fund (Treasury) and DoD to the Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund, representing the actuarially determined normal cost of future TRICARE for Life benefits being earned by current active-duty and reserve members.
beneficiaries: DoD Medicare-Eligible Retiree Health Care Fund, indirectly future military retirees who will use TRICARE for Life

name: Expenses, Medical Facilities, Veterans Health Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds the operation, maintenance, and minor construction of VA medical facilities including hospitals, clinics, nursing homes, and domiciliaries. Covers utilities, engineering, facility repair, leases, and capital equipment for VA's healthcare infrastructure.
beneficiaries: VA medical facilities, indirectly all veterans receiving care at VA healthcare facilities

name: Science, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds the Department of Energy's Office of Science, supporting basic research in physical sciences, high-energy physics, nuclear physics, biological and environmental research, advanced scientific computing, and operation of national laboratories.
beneficiaries: National laboratories (Argonne, Brookhaven, Fermilab, Oak Ridge, etc.), university researchers, scientific workforce

name: Salaries and Expenses, Federal Prison System, Justice
description: Covers the operating costs of the Federal Bureau of Prisons, including staff salaries, inmate care, food, medical services, facility maintenance, and contract confinement costs. The BOP was established by an act of Congress in 1930.
beneficiaries: Federal inmates (~155,000), BOP staff (~36,000), contract facility operators

name: Payment to States for the Child Care and Development Block Grant, Administration for Children and Families, HHS
description: Provides federal block grant funding to states, territories, and tribes to subsidize child care for low-income working families and to improve child care quality. Authorized by the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990.
beneficiaries: Low-income working families with children under 13, child care providers, state child care agencies

name: Social Security Equivalent Benefit Account, Railroad Retirement Board
description: Pays the portion of railroad retirement benefits equivalent to Social Security benefits that railroad workers would have received had their employment been covered by Social Security. Restructured under the Railroad Retirement Act of 1974.
beneficiaries: Retired railroad workers and their spouses, survivors of deceased railroad employees

name: Refugee and Entrant Assistance, Administration for Children and Families, HHS
description: Provides resettlement assistance to refugees, Cuban/Haitian entrants, asylees, and unaccompanied alien children including cash assistance, medical screening, social services, and shelter. Authorized by the Refugee Act of 1980.
beneficiaries: Refugees admitted to the U.S., asylees, unaccompanied migrant children, Cuban/Haitian entrants, voluntary resettlement agencies

name: Public Housing Fund, Public and Indian Housing, HUD
description: Provides operating subsidies and capital funds to approximately 3,000 public housing authorities nationwide for the maintenance, operation, and modernization of roughly 1 million public housing units. Traces origins to the Housing Act of 1937.
beneficiaries: Low-income tenants in public housing, public housing authorities, elderly and disabled residents

name: Indian Health Services, Indian Health Service, HHS
description: Funds health care delivery to approximately 2.6 million American Indians and Alaska Natives through a network of hospitals, clinics, and health stations operated by IHS, tribes, and urban Indian organizations. Established under the Indian Health Care Improvement Act.
beneficiaries: Members of federally recognized tribes, Alaska Natives, urban Indian populations

name: Economic Support Fund, Funds Appropriated to the President
description: Provides economic assistance to foreign countries and regions of strategic importance to U.S. interests, supporting governance, economic reform, health, education, and stabilization programs. Authorized under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961.
beneficiaries: Partner governments (Israel, Egypt, Jordan, etc.), NGOs, populations in developing and conflict-affected countries

name: Highway Infrastructure Programs, Federal Highway Administration, Transportation
description: Provides funding for highway and bridge infrastructure projects beyond the core Federal-Aid Highway Program, including competitive grants for major projects and nationally significant freight and highway projects. Expanded significantly under IIJA (2021).
beneficiaries: State departments of transportation, metropolitan planning organizations, motorists, freight carriers

name: Construction, Corps of Engineers - Civil
description: Funds construction of new water resources projects by the Army Corps of Engineers including navigation, flood and storm damage reduction, aquatic ecosystem restoration, and water supply projects. The Corps' civil works mission dates to 1824.
beneficiaries: Communities in flood-prone areas, commercial navigation interests, coastal populations, environmental habitats

name: Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds DOE's Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy (EERE), supporting R&D and deployment of solar, wind, geothermal, hydrogen, bioenergy, vehicle, building, and manufacturing energy technologies.
beneficiaries: Clean energy companies, manufacturers, homeowners, vehicle fleet operators, national laboratories, university researchers

name: Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants and Children (WIC), Food and Nutrition Service
description: Provides supplemental food packages, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and health care referrals to low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, infants, and children up to age 5 who are at nutritional risk.
beneficiaries: Low-income pregnant and postpartum women, infants, children under 5, WIC-authorized food retailers

name: Exploration, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: Funds NASA's human exploration programs including the Artemis lunar program, Space Launch System, Orion spacecraft, Gateway lunar space station, and human Mars exploration planning.
beneficiaries: NASA centers, aerospace contractors (Boeing, Lockheed Martin, SpaceX, etc.), astronaut corps, scientific community

name: International Disaster Assistance, Funds Appropriated to the President, USAID
description: Provides humanitarian assistance for international disaster relief including emergency food, shelter, water, sanitation, and health services in response to natural disasters and complex emergencies worldwide.
beneficiaries: Populations affected by natural disasters and conflicts worldwide, international humanitarian organizations (UNHCR, WFP, ICRC), NGOs

name: National Sea-Based Deterrence Fund, Navy
description: Finances the Columbia-class ballistic missile submarine (SSBN) program, the replacement for Ohio-class submarines that carry the sea-based leg of the U.S. nuclear triad. Created by the National Defense Authorization Act for FY2015.
beneficiaries: General Dynamics Electric Boat, BWX Technologies, Navy submarine fleet, nuclear deterrence infrastructure

name: Defense Environmental Cleanup, Environmental and Other Defense Activities, Energy
description: Funds cleanup of radioactive and hazardous waste contamination at former nuclear weapons production and research sites across the DOE complex, including Hanford, Savannah River, and Idaho National Laboratory.
beneficiaries: Communities near former nuclear weapons sites, environmental remediation contractors, cleanup workers, downwind/downstream populations

name: National Cancer Institute, National Institutes of Health, HHS
description: The largest institute within NIH, funding cancer research including basic, translational, and clinical studies on cancer prevention, diagnosis, and treatment. Established by the National Cancer Institute Act of 1937.
beneficiaries: Cancer researchers at universities and medical centers, cancer patients, NCI-designated cancer centers (~71), pharmaceutical/biotech companies

name: Public Health and Social Services Emergency Fund, Office of the Secretary, HHS
description: Provides funding for public health emergency preparedness and response, including BARDA (Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority), pandemic preparedness, medical countermeasure development, and the Strategic National Stockpile.
beneficiaries: State and local health departments, hospitals, pharmaceutical manufacturers, the general public during health emergencies

name: Program Management, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, HHS
description: Covers the administrative costs of running CMS, including Medicare claims processing, program integrity and fraud prevention, survey and certification of health care providers, research, and IT systems including Healthcare.gov.
beneficiaries: Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries (indirectly), CMS employees, health care providers, Medicare Administrative Contractors

name: Science, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: Funds NASA's Science Mission Directorate covering Earth science, planetary science, astrophysics, heliophysics, and biological/physical sciences, including flagship missions like the James Webb Space Telescope, Mars rovers, and Earth-observing satellites.
beneficiaries: University and NASA scientists, Jet Propulsion Laboratory, aerospace contractors, the global scientific community

name: Operations, Research and Facilities, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Commerce
description: Funds NOAA's core operations including the National Weather Service, satellite systems, ocean and coastal research, fisheries management, marine sanctuaries, and climate research. NOAA was established in 1970.
beneficiaries: General public (weather forecasts), fishing industry, coastal communities, aviation/maritime sectors, climate researchers

name: Grants to States for Medicaid, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, HHS
description: This is the administrative/survey and certification component of Medicaid grants to states, separate from the much larger mandatory benefit payments. It supports state Medicaid program administration, eligibility determination, and provider oversight.
beneficiaries: State Medicaid agencies, low-income Medicaid enrollees (indirectly), health care providers participating in Medicaid

name: Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation Fund, PBGC, Labor
description: Operates as a federal corporation that insures defined-benefit pension plans in the private sector, paying benefits to retirees when their employer pension plans fail. Created by ERISA (Employee Retirement Income Security Act) of 1974.
beneficiaries: Workers and retirees whose defined-benefit pension plans have been terminated, companies with underfunded pension plans

name: Procurement of Weapons and Tracked Combat Vehicles, Army
description: Funds the Army's procurement of tanks (M1 Abrams upgrades), Bradley Fighting Vehicles, Stryker combat vehicles, artillery systems, small arms, ammunition, and other weapons systems and combat vehicle modifications.
beneficiaries: Defense contractors (General Dynamics, BAE Systems, Oshkosh Defense), Army combat units, Army depots

name: Salaries and Expenses, Food and Drug Administration, HHS
description: Funds FDA operations including regulation of food safety, drug and biologic approvals, medical device oversight, tobacco regulation, and cosmetics safety. The FDA traces its regulatory origins to the Pure Food and Drug Act of 1906.
beneficiaries: Consumers (food and drug safety), pharmaceutical companies (regulatory pathway), medical device manufacturers, food industry

name: Refunds and Drawbacks, United States Customs Service (Indefinite), Treasury
description: Provides for refunds of customs duties, taxes, and fees that were overpaid, collected in error, or eligible for drawback (refund of duties on imported goods that are subsequently exported). An indefinite appropriation tied to customs law since the founding era.
beneficiaries: Importers and exporters, customs brokers, manufacturers who re-export imported materials

name: Information Technology Systems, Departmental Administration, Veterans Affairs
description: Funds the VA's Office of Information and Technology for enterprise-wide IT systems including the electronic health record modernization (Oracle Cerner), VistA, cybersecurity, telehealth platforms, and benefits processing systems.
beneficiaries: Veterans (through improved service delivery), VA employees, IT contractors (Oracle/Cerner, etc.)

name: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases, NIH, HHS
description: Funds research on infectious, immunologic, and allergic diseases including HIV/AIDS, influenza, emerging infectious diseases, biodefense, and autoimmune conditions. NIAID conducts and supports both basic and clinical research.
beneficiaries: Biomedical researchers, patients with infectious and immune-mediated diseases, vaccine and therapeutic developers

name: Farm Security and Rural Investment Programs, Natural Resources Conservation Service, Agriculture
description: Funds NRCS conservation programs authorized under successive farm bills, including EQIP (Environmental Quality Incentives Program), CSP (Conservation Stewardship Program), and ACEP (Agricultural Conservation Easement Program).
beneficiaries: Farmers and ranchers implementing conservation practices, private forest landowners, tribal agricultural producers

name: Defense Working Capital Fund, Defense Commissary Agency
description: Operates the Defense Commissary Agency which runs approximately 236 commissary stores worldwide, providing groceries and household goods to military members, retirees, and their families at cost plus a 5% surcharge.
beneficiaries: Active-duty military, military retirees, military families, reserve/guard members, commissary employees

name: Operations Support, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds IRS operational support functions including IT infrastructure, telecommunications, rent and facilities, printing, postage, security, and shared services. Established as a separate account in the IRS restructuring of appropriation accounts.
beneficiaries: IRS employees, U.S. taxpayers (through improved tax administration), IT service providers

name: Federal-State Partnership for Intercity Passenger Rail Grants, Federal Railroad Administration, Transportation
description: Provides competitive grants for capital projects to reduce the state of good repair backlog, improve performance, and expand intercity passenger rail service, particularly on the Northeast Corridor. Created by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) of 2021.
beneficiaries: Amtrak, state transportation agencies, commuter rail operators, intercity rail passengers, NEC communities

name: Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, HHS
description: A Treasury-managed component of the Medicare Part B and Part D trust fund that handles interest payments, transfers, and certain administrative operations related to the Supplementary Medical Insurance program established by Medicare in 1965.
beneficiaries: Medicare Part B and Part D beneficiaries, health care providers, prescription drug plan sponsors

name: Primary Health Care, Health Resources and Services Administration, HHS
description: Funds the Health Center Program (Section 330 grants) supporting approximately 1,400 community health centers that provide primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services to underserved communities regardless of ability to pay.
beneficiaries: Uninsured and underinsured patients, Medicaid enrollees, community health centers (FQHCs), rural and urban underserved populations

name: Operation and Maintenance, Corps of Engineers - Civil
description: Funds the ongoing operation and maintenance of existing Army Corps civil works infrastructure including locks, dams, levees, harbors, waterways, hydropower plants, recreation areas, and regulatory permits programs.
beneficiaries: Inland waterway navigation industry, communities protected by flood control infrastructure, hydropower consumers, recreation users

name: Operation and Maintenance, Corps of Engineers, Civil
description: Duplicate entry for the Corps of Engineers O&M account (slightly different naming convention). Supports the same dredging, lock operations, dam safety, levee maintenance, and environmental stewardship activities.
beneficiaries: Inland waterway navigation industry, communities protected by flood infrastructure, hydropower consumers

name: Working Capital Fund, State
description: A revolving fund that provides centralized administrative services to the State Department and other agencies on a reimbursable basis, including IT, logistics, procurement, financial services, and overseas building operations support.
beneficiaries: State Department bureaus and posts, other federal agencies using State Department shared services

name: Railroad Retirement Account, Railroad Retirement Board
description: Pays the industry-funded portion of railroad retirement benefits (Tier II) above the Social Security-equivalent amount, plus supplemental annuities, spouse/survivor benefits, and unemployment/sickness insurance for railroad workers.
beneficiaries: Retired railroad workers, their spouses and survivors, disabled railroad employees

name: Wildland Fire Management, Forest Service, Agriculture
description: Funds the U.S. Forest Service's wildfire suppression, fuels management (prescribed burns, mechanical treatments), preparedness, and post-fire rehabilitation activities across 193 million acres of national forests and grasslands.
beneficiaries: Communities in the wildland-urban interface, firefighters, national forest ecosystems, state and local fire agencies

name: Immigration Examination Fees, Immigration Services, Homeland Security
description: A fee-funded account supporting U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) operations including processing of immigration benefit applications such as naturalization, green cards, work permits, asylum, and refugee status adjustments.
beneficiaries: Immigration applicants (visa, green card, citizenship), employers sponsoring workers, USCIS employees

name: Community Development Fund, Community Planning and Development, HUD
description: Primarily funds the Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) program, providing formula grants to states and local governments for housing, infrastructure, economic development, and public services in low- and moderate-income communities. Authorized by the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974.
beneficiaries: Low- and moderate-income communities, local governments, state agencies, community development organizations

name: Migration and Refugee Assistance, State
description: Funds U.S. contributions to international refugee protection and assistance through UNHCR, the International Committee of the Red Cross, the International Organization for Migration, and other partners providing protection and durable solutions for refugees worldwide.
beneficiaries: Refugees worldwide, UNHCR, ICRC, IOM, international and local NGOs providing refugee services

name: Operation and Maintenance, Space Force
description: Funds the day-to-day operations of the U.S. Space Force including satellite operations, space domain awareness, launch operations, cyber operations, facility sustainment, and civilian personnel. The Space Force was established in December 2019.
beneficiaries: Space Force guardians and civilian employees, space operations centers, satellite ground stations, all military services dependent on space capabilities

name: Refunding Internal Revenue Collections, Interest (Indefinite), Treasury
description: An indefinite/permanent appropriation covering interest payments the government owes to taxpayers on overpaid taxes that were refunded after a statutory period, as required by the Internal Revenue Code.
beneficiaries: U.S. taxpayers receiving refunds with interest, primarily corporate taxpayers with large overpayments

name: Affordable Connectivity Fund, Federal Communications Commission
description: Provided $30/month broadband subsidies (up to $75 on tribal lands) to eligible low-income households to help afford internet service. Created by the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (2021); funding was largely exhausted by mid-2024.
beneficiaries: Low-income households, households on tribal lands, participating internet service providers

name: Payments to States for Child Support Enforcement and Family Support Programs, ACF, HHS
description: Provides federal matching funds to states for operating child support enforcement programs that locate absent parents, establish paternity, establish and enforce child support orders, and collect child support payments. Created under Title IV-D of the Social Security Act.
beneficiaries: Custodial parents and children owed support, state child support enforcement agencies, non-custodial parents (through income withholding systems)

name: Federal Assistance, Federal Emergency Management Agency, Homeland Security
description: Funds FEMA's non-disaster grant programs including the State Homeland Security Grant Program, Urban Area Security Initiative, port and transit security grants, Emergency Management Performance Grants, and firefighter assistance grants.
beneficiaries: State and local emergency management agencies, fire departments, law enforcement, port authorities, transit agencies

name: Enforcement, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds IRS enforcement activities including audits, investigations, tax litigation, criminal enforcement, document matching, and collection of delinquent taxes. Separated into its own account as part of IRS appropriation restructuring.
beneficiaries: U.S. taxpayers (through improved compliance), the federal government (through increased revenue collection)

name: Consular and Border Security Programs, Administration of Foreign Affairs, State
description: Funds the State Department's consular operations including visa adjudication, passport issuance, American citizen services abroad, and border security screening programs. Largely fee-funded through passport and visa fees.
beneficiaries: U.S. passport applicants, foreign visa applicants, U.S. citizens abroad requiring emergency services

name: Safety, Security and Mission Services, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: Funds NASA's institutional operations including center management, safety and mission assurance, protective services, IT infrastructure, environmental compliance, and agency-wide mission support services across NASA's facilities.
beneficiaries: NASA centers and employees, NASA contractors providing institutional support services

name: Electricity, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds DOE's Office of Electricity, supporting research and development on grid modernization, energy storage, transmission infrastructure, cybersecurity for the electric grid, and transformer reserves. Significantly expanded under IIJA.
beneficiaries: Electric utilities, grid operators, energy storage companies, electricity consumers, rural electric cooperatives

name: Processing, Research and Marketing, Office of the Secretary, Agriculture
description: Funds USDA activities related to agricultural commodity processing, marketing services, grading and inspection, agricultural research coordination, and market access programs that support American agricultural producers.
beneficiaries: Agricultural producers, food processors, exporters of agricultural commodities, consumers

name: National Institute on Aging, National Institutes of Health, HHS
description: Funds research on aging processes, age-related diseases including Alzheimer's disease and related dementias, and the biological, social, and behavioral aspects of aging. Established by the Research on Aging Act of 1974.
beneficiaries: Older Americans, Alzheimer's disease patients and caregivers, aging researchers, Alzheimer's Disease Research Centers

name: Postal Service Contributions for Retiree Health Benefits, Office of Personnel Management
description: Covers the federal government's required contributions for Postal Service retiree health benefits under the Federal Employees Health Benefits Program. Requirements were restructured by the Postal Service Reform Act of 2022.
beneficiaries: Postal Service retirees enrolled in FEHB, the Postal Service Retiree Health Benefits Fund

name: Aircraft Procurement, Army
description: Funds procurement and modification of Army rotary-wing and fixed-wing aircraft including Black Hawk helicopters, Apache attack helicopters, Chinook cargo helicopters, and Future Vertical Lift aircraft.
beneficiaries: Army aviation units, defense contractors (Sikorsky/Lockheed Martin, Boeing), Army depots, aviation maintenance workforce

name: Procurement, Space Force, Air Force
description: Funds procurement of Space Force systems including missile warning satellites, GPS satellites, protected communications satellites, space launch services, and ground-based space surveillance systems.
beneficiaries: Space Force, satellite manufacturers (Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, L3Harris), launch providers (SpaceX, ULA)

name: National Network Grants to the National Railroad Passenger Corporation, Transportation
description: Provides federal grants to Amtrak for its national long-distance route network operating and capital needs, supporting approximately 15 long-distance routes serving over 500 communities across 46 states. Restructured under IIJA (2021).
beneficiaries: Amtrak, intercity rail passengers especially in rural communities, Amtrak employees

name: Development Assistance, Agency for International Development, Funds Appropriated to the President
description: Funds USAID's core development programs in agriculture, education, health, democracy and governance, economic growth, and environment in developing countries. Authorized under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961.
beneficiaries: Populations in developing countries, NGOs, USAID implementing partners, host-country governments and institutions

name: National Flood Insurance Fund, Federal Emergency Management Agency, Homeland Security
description: Operates the National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP), providing federally backed flood insurance policies to homeowners, renters, and businesses in participating communities. Created by the National Flood Insurance Act of 1968.
beneficiaries: Property owners in flood-prone areas, mortgage holders, floodplain communities, Write-Your-Own insurance companies

name: Space Operations, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: Funds NASA's human spaceflight operations including the International Space Station, commercial crew transportation (SpaceX Crew Dragon, Boeing Starliner), the Space Communications and Navigation network, and launch services.
beneficiaries: ISS partners and researchers, commercial space companies (SpaceX, Boeing), NASA astronauts, space communications users

name: Mutual Mortgage Insurance Program Account, Federal Housing Administration, HUD
description: Operates FHA's single-family mortgage insurance program, insuring home loans made by approved lenders to borrowers who may not qualify for conventional mortgages, particularly first-time and low-to-moderate income homebuyers. Created by the National Housing Act of 1934.
beneficiaries: First-time homebuyers, low-to-moderate income borrowers, FHA-approved mortgage lenders

name: Salaries and Expenses, United States Patent and Trademark Office, Commerce
description: Funds USPTO operations including examination and issuance of patents and registration of trademarks. Almost entirely fee-funded by patent and trademark applicants. The modern USPTO structure dates to the Patent Act of 1836.
beneficiaries: Patent and trademark applicants, inventors, businesses protecting intellectual property, patent examiners

name: Substance Abuse Treatment, Substance Abuse and Mental Health Administration, HHS
description: Funds SAMHSA's substance abuse prevention and treatment programs including the Substance Abuse Prevention and Treatment Block Grant, opioid treatment programs, and targeted capacity expansion grants. SAMHSA was created in 1992.
beneficiaries: Individuals with substance use disorders, state substance abuse agencies, treatment providers, communities affected by opioid crisis

name: Rehabilitation Services, Education
description: Funds state vocational rehabilitation programs that help individuals with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment, plus independent living services, client assistance programs, and supported employment grants. Authorized under the Rehabilitation Act of 1973.
beneficiaries: Individuals with physical and mental disabilities, state vocational rehabilitation agencies, independent living centers

name: Taxpayer Services, Internal Revenue Service, Treasury
description: Funds IRS taxpayer-facing services including processing of tax returns, providing taxpayer assistance (phone, walk-in, online), issuing refunds, pre-filing assistance, and the Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program.
beneficiaries: U.S. taxpayers (individual and business), tax preparers, low-income filers using free file and VITA programs

name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance, Administration for Children and Families, HHS
description: Provides block grants to states to help low-income households pay heating and cooling bills, weatherize homes, and address energy-related emergencies. Created by the Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Act of 1981 (LIHEAP).
beneficiaries: Low-income households, elderly persons on fixed incomes, disabled individuals, families with young children

name: Training and Employment Service, Employment and Training Administration, Labor
description: Funds workforce development programs under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA), including adult and dislocated worker training, youth employment programs, Job Corps, YouthBuild, and apprenticeship programs.
beneficiaries: Unemployed and underemployed workers, dislocated workers, at-risk youth, Job Corps participants, American Job Centers

name: State Unemployment Insurance and Employment Service Operations, Employment and Training Administration, Labor
description: Provides federal funding to states for administration of unemployment insurance programs, employment services (job matching, career counseling), and labor market information systems. Rooted in the Social Security Act of 1935 and the Wagner-Peyser Act of 1933.
beneficiaries: Unemployed workers filing UI claims, job seekers using employment services, state workforce agencies

name: Federal Financing Bank Revolving Fund, Treasury
description: Operates the Federal Financing Bank, a government corporation that purchases obligations issued or guaranteed by other federal agencies, providing them with lower-cost financing by borrowing from the Treasury. Created by the Federal Financing Bank Act of 1973.
beneficiaries: Federal agencies and federally-backed borrowers (rural utilities, student loan programs, export financing)

name: Environmental Programs and Management, Environmental Protection Agency
