description: EPA's primary operating account funding regulatory and compliance programs across air quality, water quality, hazardous waste management, pesticides, toxic substances, environmental justice, and enforcement activities. EPA was created in 1970.
beneficiaries: General public (environmental and health protection), regulated industries, state environmental agencies, environmental justice communities

name: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute, National Institutes of Health, HHS
description: Funds research on cardiovascular, pulmonary, blood, and sleep disorders, and manages the nation's blood supply research programs. Originally the National Heart Institute (1948), expanded to cover lung and blood diseases in 1972.
beneficiaries: Patients with heart disease, lung disease, blood disorders, and sleep disorders; cardiovascular researchers; clinical trial participants

name: Water and Related Resources, Bureau of Reclamation, Interior
description: Funds the Bureau of Reclamation's water infrastructure projects in the 17 western states, including dam and reservoir operations, water delivery systems, drought contingency, desalination, water recycling, and rural water projects. Reclamation was established by the Reclamation Act of 1902.
beneficiaries: Western farmers and irrigation districts, municipal water users, hydroelectric power consumers, tribal water settlements, endangered species

name: Working Capital Fund, International Cooperative Administrative Support Services, State
description: A revolving fund that provides shared administrative support services (such as facility maintenance, IT, shipping, local staff management) at U.S. embassies overseas, with costs shared among all U.S. government agencies present at each post.
beneficiaries: U.S. government agencies with overseas presence (State, USAID, DoD, Commerce, etc.), embassy staff

name: Homeless Assistance Grants, Community Planning and Development, HUD
description: Funds competitive and formula grants for homeless assistance including the Continuum of Care program, Emergency Solutions Grants, and rural homelessness programs. Provides funding for shelters, transitional housing, permanent supportive housing, and homelessness prevention. Originated under the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act of 1987.
beneficiaries: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness, homeless service providers, Continuums of Care, local governments

name: Special Benefits, Office of Workers Compensation Program, Labor
description: Provides workers' compensation benefits to federal civilian employees and certain other workers injured on the job, covering medical treatment, wage replacement, vocational rehabilitation, and death benefits under the Federal Employees' Compensation Act (FECA).
beneficiaries: Injured federal civilian employees, surviving dependents of federal workers killed on duty, postal workers

name: Employees' Life Insurance Fund, Office of Personnel Management
description: Operates the Federal Employees' Group Life Insurance (FEGLI) program, providing group term life insurance coverage to approximately 4 million federal employees and retirees. Established by the Federal Employees' Group Life Insurance Act of 1954.
beneficiaries: Federal employees and retirees enrolled in FEGLI, their designated beneficiaries and survivors

name: Payments to States from Receipts Under Mineral Leasing, Public and Acquired Military Lands, Office of Natural Resources Revenue, Interior
description: Distributes to states their statutory share (typically 50%) of revenues collected from mineral extraction (oil, gas, coal, etc.) on federal and acquired military lands within their borders. Mandated by the Mineral Leasing Act of 1920.
beneficiaries: State governments (especially Wyoming, New Mexico, Colorado, Utah, North Dakota), schools, and roads funded by mineral revenues

name: Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund - Treasury Managed, HHS
description: A Treasury-managed component of the Medicare Part A (Hospital Insurance) trust fund, handling interest earnings, investment management, and certain administrative transfers for the HI trust fund established by Medicare legislation in 1965.
beneficiaries: Medicare Part A beneficiaries (those 65+ and certain disabled), hospitals, skilled nursing facilities

name: Capital Investments Grants, Federal Transit Administration, Transportation
description: Provides competitive federal funding for major transit capital investments including new fixed guideway systems (light rail, heavy rail, bus rapid transit), extensions, and core capacity improvements in existing systems. Known as the New Starts/Small Starts/Core Capacity program.
beneficiaries: Transit agencies building new rail and BRT systems, commuters in metropolitan areas, construction firms

name: Embassy Security, Construction, and Maintenance, State
description: Funds the construction, acquisition, maintenance, and security upgrades of U.S. diplomatic facilities worldwide. Created in response to the 1998 embassy bombings in Kenya and Tanzania that highlighted the need for more secure diplomatic compounds.
beneficiaries: State Department employees overseas, U.S. diplomatic missions, construction and security contractors

name: Child Care Entitlement to States, Administration for Children and Families, HHS
description: Provides mandatory/entitlement funding to states for child care assistance to low-income families, particularly those transitioning from welfare to work. Created under the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996 (welfare reform).
beneficiaries: Low-income families receiving or transitioning off TANF, working-poor families, child care providers

name: Higher Education, Department of Education
description: Funds federal higher education programs including TRIO (upward bound, talent search, student support services), GEAR UP, Title III/V institutional aid to minority-serving institutions (HBCUs, HSIs, Tribal Colleges), and international education. Authorized under the Higher Education Act of 1965.
beneficiaries: Low-income and first-generation college students, HBCUs, Hispanic-Serving Institutions, Tribal Colleges, TRIO/GEAR UP participants

name: Bonneville Power Administration Fund, Power Marketing Administration, Energy
description: Operates the Bonneville Power Administration, which markets wholesale electric power from 31 federal hydroelectric dams on the Columbia River system in the Pacific Northwest, plus transmission over 15,000 miles of high-voltage lines.
beneficiaries: Pacific Northwest electric utilities, industrial customers, ratepayers in OR/WA/ID/MT, salmon recovery programs

name: SPR Petroleum Account, Energy Programs, Energy
description: Funds the operation, maintenance, and petroleum acquisition for the Strategic Petroleum Reserve, the world's largest government-owned emergency crude oil stockpile stored in underground salt caverns along the Gulf Coast. Created by the Energy Policy and Conservation Act of 1975.
beneficiaries: U.S. consumers and economy (energy security), oil markets during supply disruptions, Gulf Coast storage facility workers

name: Grants-In-Aid for Airports, Liquidation of Contract Authorization, Airport and Airway Trust Fund, FAA, Transportation
description: Provides federal grants to public-use airports for planning, construction, and improvement of runways, taxiways, terminals, and safety/security infrastructure through the Airport Improvement Program (AIP). Funded by the Airport and Airway Trust Fund from aviation excise taxes.
beneficiaries: Public-use airports (especially small and rural), airport operators, airline passengers, general aviation users

name: Safety, Security and Missions Services, National Aeronautics and Space Administration
description: Duplicate entry (slight naming variation) for NASA's institutional and mission support account. Covers center operations, environmental remediation, security, agency IT, and construction of facilities across NASA's 10 field centers.
beneficiaries: NASA centers and employees, facility maintenance and construction contractors

name: Capital Investment Grants, Federal Transit Administration, Transportation
description: Duplicate entry (slight naming variation) for FTA capital investment grants. Supports the project development pipeline for major transit projects through engineering, final design, and full funding grant agreement stages.
beneficiaries: Transit agencies, metropolitan commuters, communities along new transit corridors

name: Hazardous Substance Superfund - Treasury Managed, Environmental Protection Agency
description: Funds the cleanup of the nation's most contaminated hazardous waste sites (Superfund sites) on the National Priorities List. Created by CERCLA (Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act) of 1980, commonly known as Superfund.
beneficiaries: Communities near contaminated sites, environmental remediation contractors, state environmental agencies, public health (reduced toxic exposure)

name: Facilities and Equipment, Airport and Airway Trust Fund, FAA, Transportation
description: Funds FAA capital investments in the air traffic control system including radar and surveillance equipment, navigation aids, communication systems, weather systems, automation, and NextGen air traffic modernization technologies.
beneficiaries: Airlines, general aviation, air traffic controllers, airline passengers, aviation safety

name: National Institute of General Medical Sciences, National Institutes of Health, HHS
description: Funds basic biomedical research that is not targeted at specific diseases, including cell biology, genetics, biophysics, pharmacology, and computational biology. Also administers the IDeA program supporting research in states with historically low NIH funding.
beneficiaries: Basic science researchers at universities, graduate students and postdocs, IDeA-eligible states (primarily rural and underserved)

name: United States Mint Public Enterprise Fund, Treasury
description: Operates the U.S. Mint as a public enterprise revolving fund, covering all costs of manufacturing and distributing circulating coins, bullion coins (American Eagle, Buffalo), numismatic products, and Congressional gold medals. Established as a PEF by the United States Mint Reauthorization Act of 1995.
beneficiaries: Federal Reserve Banks (circulating coin supply), coin collectors, bullion investors, the general public
